-- Create a configurable quarter function: start_month is 1..12 (e.g., 4 for April)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(date_in date, start_month int)
RETURNS int
LANGUAGE SQL
IMMUTABLE
STRICT
AS $$
  SELECT 1 + (((EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date_in)::int - start_month + 12) % 12) / 3);
$$;

-- Example: April-based quarters (Apr-Jun = Q1, Jul-Sep = Q2, ...)

        'bonds', COALESCE((

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Start from heads: cards that no other card points to
  SELECT
    c.id AS start_id,
    c.id,
    c.nextcardid,
    c.id::text AS path
  FROM collection c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM collection p WHERE p.nextcardid = c.id)

  UNION ALL

  -- Follow the chain forward
  SELECT
    path_cte.start_id,
    n.id,
    n.nextcardid,
    path_cte.path || ' --> ' || n.id::text
  FROM path_cte
  JOIN collection n
    ON n.id = path_cte.nextcardid
)
-- Keep only complete paths that end at a tail (nextcardid IS NULL), ordered by the head id
SELECT path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextcardid IS NULL
ORDER BY start_id;

-- Add the target column
ALTER TABLE "trans" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS next_bank text;

-- Optional but recommended index to speed up the lookup of the next non-null bank
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_trans_acc_date_id_bank_notnull
  ON "trans" (account_id, "date", trans_id)
  WHERE bank IS NOT NULL;

-- Populate next_bank with the next non-null bank within the same account,
-- ordered by date (and trans_id to break ties on the same date)
UPDATE "trans" t
SET next_bank = (
  SELECT t2.bank
  FROM "trans" t2
  WHERE t2.account_id = t.account_id
    AND t2.bank IS NOT NULL
    AND (
      t2."date" > t."date"
      OR (t2."date" = t."date" AND t2.trans_id > t.trans_id)
    )
  ORDER BY t2."date", t2.trans_id
  LIMIT 1
);

WITH months AS (
  -- Normalize to one row per user per month (first day of month as a date)
  SELECT DISTINCT
         user_id,
         to_date(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS month_date
  FROM purchase
  WHERE purchase_date ~ '^\d{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$'
),
seq AS (
  -- Assign a sequence number and a month index to each user's months
  SELECT
    user_id,
    month_date,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) AS rn,
    (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM month_date)::int * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM month_date)::int) AS month_num
  FROM months
),
streaks AS (
  -- Consecutive months share the same (month_num - rn) key; count lengths of each run
  SELECT
    user_id,
    (month_num - rn)::int AS grp_key,
    COUNT(*) AS streak_length
  FROM seq
  GROUP BY user_id, (month_num - rn)::int
),
longest AS (
  -- Longest streak per user
  SELECT user_id, MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
  FROM streaks
  GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT
  longest_streak AS streak_length,
  COUNT(*) AS user_count
FROM longest
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length DESC;

-- Match rows where the tag array contains at least one element matching EACH wildcard pattern
-- Replace the array literal below with your patterns (e.g., ARRAY['%friend%','%cat%'])
SELECT DISTINCT pt.userid, pt.tag
FROM preference_tag AS pt
WHERE (
  SELECT COUNT(*) 
  FROM unnest(ARRAY['%friend%', '%cat%']::text[]) AS patt(p)
  WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM unnest(pt.tag::text[]) AS t(elem)
    WHERE t.elem ILIKE patt.p
  )
) = CARDINALITY(ARRAY['%friend%', '%cat%']::text[]);

SELECT
  bt.relname AS base_table,
  bcol.attname AS base_col,
  rt.relname AS referencing_table,
  rcol.attname AS referencing_col,
  'CONSTRAINT ' || quote_ident(c.conname) || ' ' || pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint AS c
JOIN pg_class AS bt ON bt.oid = c.confrelid            -- referenced (base) table
JOIN pg_class AS rt ON rt.oid = c.conrelid             -- referencing (child) table
JOIN generate_subscripts(c.conkey, 1) AS ord(i) ON true
JOIN pg_attribute AS rcol
  ON rcol.attrelid = c.conrelid
 AND rcol.attnum   = c.conkey[ord.i]
 AND NOT rcol.attisdropped
JOIN pg_attribute AS bcol
  ON bcol.attrelid = c.confrelid
 AND bcol.attnum   = c.confkey[ord.i]
 AND NOT bcol.attisdropped
WHERE c.contype = 'f'
  AND c.confrelid = 'users'::regclass
ORDER BY referencing_table, c.conname, ord.i;

WITH per_side AS (
  SELECT
    m.home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM m."date"::timestamp)::int AS match_hour,
    m.home_team_goal AS goals_scored,
    'home' AS side
  FROM "match" m
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    m.away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM m."date"::timestamp)::int AS match_hour,
    m.away_team_goal AS goals_scored,
    'away' AS side
  FROM "match" m
)
SELECT
  team_api_id,
  match_hour,
  AVG(goals_scored) AS avg_goals_scored,
  AVG(goals_scored) FILTER (WHERE side = 'home') AS avg_home_goals,
  AVG(goals_scored) FILTER (WHERE side = 'away') AS avg_away_goals,
  SUM(goals_scored) AS total_goals_scored,
  SUM(goals_scored) FILTER (WHERE side = 'home') AS total_home_goals,
  SUM(goals_scored) FILTER (WHERE side = 'away') AS total_away_goals,
  MAX(goals_scored) AS max_goals_scored,
  MIN(goals_scored) AS min_goals_scored,
  COUNT(*) AS matches_played
FROM per_side
GROUP BY team_api_id, match_hour
ORDER BY team_api_id, match_hour;

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    hero_id,
    mission_start,
    mission_end,
    CASE
      WHEN LAG(mission_end) OVER (
             PARTITION BY hero_id
             ORDER BY mission_start, mission_end
           ) = mission_start
      THEN 0
      ELSE 1
    END AS grp_start
  FROM superhero_missions
),
grouped AS (
  SELECT
    hero_id,
    mission_start,
    mission_end,
    SUM(grp_start) OVER (
      PARTITION BY hero_id
      ORDER BY mission_start, mission_end
      ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
    ) AS grp
  FROM ordered
)
SELECT
  hero_id,
  MIN(mission_start) AS span_start,
  MAX(mission_end)   AS span_end
FROM grouped
GROUP BY hero_id, grp

-- Function: recompute and upsert the count of loans with status 'A' for the affected account
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION total_loans()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Only proceed if the status actually changed
  IF NEW.status IS NOT DISTINCT FROM OLD.status THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  -- If the updated row has no account, nothing to update
  IF NEW.account_id IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  -- Recompute count for the NEW.account_id and upsert into loan_summary
  INSERT INTO loan_summary AS ls (account_id, total_loan_count, last_updated, last_status_change)
  SELECT NEW.account_id,
         (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan l WHERE l.account_id = NEW.account_id AND l.status = 'A'),
         now(),
         CURRENT_DATE
  ON CONFLICT (account_id) DO UPDATE
  SET total_loan_count   = EXCLUDED.total_loan_count,
      last_updated       = now(),
      last_status_change = CURRENT_DATE;

  -- If the account_id also changed in the same UPDATE, refresh the OLD.account_id summary too
  IF OLD.account_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.account_id AND OLD.account_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    INSERT INTO loan_summary AS ls (account_id, total_loan_count, last_updated, last_status_change)
    SELECT OLD.account_id,
           (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan l WHERE l.account_id = OLD.account_id AND l.status = 'A'),
           now(),
           CURRENT_DATE
    ON CONFLICT (account_id) DO UPDATE
    SET total_loan_count   = EXCLUDED.total_loan_count,
        last_updated       = now(),
        last_status_change = CURRENT_DATE;
  END IF;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;

-- Trigger: fire after status changes on loan rows
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS tr_total_loans ON loan;

CREATE TRIGGER tr_total_loans
AFTER UPDATE OF status ON loan
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION total_loans();

-- Move the card with disp_id = 41 to position 1 within its type,

  SELECT type
  FROM card
  WHERE disp_id = 41
  LIMIT 1
)
UPDATE card AS c
SET disp_id = CASE
  WHEN c.disp_id = 41 THEN 1
  WHEN c.disp_id BETWEEN 1 AND 40 THEN c.disp_id + 1
  ELSE c.disp_id
END
WHERE c.type = (SELECT type FROM target_type)
  AND (c.disp_id = 41 OR c.disp_id BETWEEN 1 AND 40);

-- Single query that returns both "extra" (in schema, not required) and "missing" (required, not in schema)
with schema_tables as (
  select table_name
  from information_schema.tables
  where table_schema = 'public'
)
select
  case
    when rt.table_name is null then 'extra'   -- present in schema, not in required_tables
    when st.table_name is null then 'missing' -- present in required_tables, not in schema
  end as kind,
  coalesce(rt.table_name, st.table_name) as table_name
from required_tables rt
full join schema_tables st
  on rt.table_name = st.table_name
where rt.table_name is null or st.table_name is null
order by kind, table_name;

WITH changes AS (
  -- One row per entity and timestamp, pivoting height and weight into columns
  SELECT
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    max((value #>> '{}')::numeric) FILTER (WHERE column_id = 'height') AS height,
    max((value #>> '{}')::numeric) FILTER (WHERE column_id = 'weight') AS weight
  FROM player_stats_changes
  WHERE column_id IN ('height','weight')
  GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
),
grp AS (
  -- Build "groups" that increment when a new non-null value appears
  SELECT
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    height,
    weight,
    sum(CASE WHEN height IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS height_grp,
    sum(CASE WHEN weight IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS weight_grp
  FROM changes
)
SELECT
  entity_id,
  updated_at,
  -- Forward-fill the last known non-null value within each group
  max(height) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, height_grp) AS height,
  max(weight) OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, weight_grp) AS weight
FROM grp
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at;

-- Forward-fill all nullable columns of a table, partitioned by an ID column and ordered by a given column
-- Usage example:
-- SELECT f_gap_fill_update('public.trans', 'account_id', 'date');

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(p_tbl regclass, p_id_col text, p_order_col text)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $func$
DECLARE
    v_schema   text;
    v_table    text;
    v_id_col   text := quote_ident(p_id_col);
    v_ord_col  text := quote_ident(p_order_col);
    v_col      text;
    v_sql      text;
BEGIN
    -- Resolve schema/table from regclass
    SELECT n.nspname, c.relname
    INTO v_schema, v_table
    FROM pg_class c
    JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
    WHERE c.oid = p_tbl;

    IF v_schema IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Table % not found', p_tbl;
    END IF;

    -- Loop over all nullable columns except the partition and order columns
    FOR v_col IN
        SELECT quote_ident(a.attname)
        FROM pg_attribute a
        WHERE a.attrelid = p_tbl
          AND a.attnum > 0
          AND NOT a.attisdropped
          AND NOT a.attnotnull
          AND a.attname NOT IN (p_id_col, p_order_col)
    LOOP
        -- Forward fill using a window-based group per column:
        -- 1) grp = running count of non-null occurrences of the column within each partition.
        -- 2) For each grp, max(col) (or min(col)) reproduces the last non-null value for that run.
        v_sql := format($sql$
            UPDATE %1$I.%2$I AS t
            SET %3$s = src.ff
            FROM (
                SELECT ctid,
                       max(%3$s) OVER (PARTITION BY %4$s, grp) AS ff
                FROM (
                    SELECT ctid,
                           %4$s,
                           %5$s,
                           %3$s,
                           sum(CASE WHEN %3$s IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
                               OVER (PARTITION BY %4$s ORDER BY %5$s NULLS FIRST, ctid) AS grp
                    FROM %1$I.%2$I
                ) q
            ) AS src
            WHERE src.ctid = t.ctid
              -- Only update when the value would change (preserve existing non-nulls)
              AND t.%3$s IS DISTINCT FROM src.ff;
        $sql$, v_schema, v_table, v_col, v_id_col, v_ord_col);

        EXECUTE v_sql;
    END LOOP;
END
$func$;

  FROM attendance

-- Assuming the attendee count for an event is stored in budget.amount
-- on a specific "Attendance"-type budget row. Adjust the category filter if needed.
budget_attendee_counts AS (
  SELECT
    link_to_event,
    MAX(amount) AS oldCount
  FROM budget
  WHERE category IN ('Attendance', 'Attendees', 'Attendee Count')
  GROUP BY link_to_event
),
all_events AS (
  SELECT link_to_event FROM attendance
  UNION
  SELECT link_to_event FROM budget WHERE category IN ('Attendance', 'Attendees', 'Attendee Count')
)
SELECT
  e.link_to_event,
  a.newCount AS newCount,
  b.oldCount AS oldCount
FROM all_events e
LEFT JOIN attendance_counts a ON a.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
LEFT JOIN budget_attendee_counts b ON b.link_to_event = e.link_to_event
WHERE a.newCount <> b.oldCount
   OR a.newCount IS NULL
   OR b.oldCount IS NULL;

    t.*,
    -- Build a proper timestamp from date + time text
    (t.date::timestamp + COALESCE(NULLIF(t."time", '')::time, time '00:00:00')) AS ts
  FROM transactions_1k t
),
-- Keep only the last transaction per (customer, gas station)
last_per_station AS (
  SELECT b.*
  FROM (
    SELECT
      b.*,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY b.customerid, b.gasstationid
        ORDER BY b.ts DESC, b.transactionid DESC
      ) AS rn
    FROM base b
  ) s
  WHERE s.rn = 1
),
-- Among last-per-station rows, find the earliest issue (<10) per customer
first_issue AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (customerid)
    customerid, transactionid, gasstationid, amount, ts
  FROM last_per_station
  WHERE amount < 10
  ORDER BY customerid, ts ASC, transactionid ASC
),
-- Fallback: if no issue exists for a customer, take the latest non-issue (>=10)
last_good AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (customerid)
    customerid, transactionid, gasstationid, amount, ts
  FROM last_per_station
  WHERE amount >= 10
  ORDER BY customerid, ts DESC, transactionid DESC
)
-- Prefer the first issue if it exists; otherwise return the last good
SELECT
  COALESCE(fi.customerid, lg.customerid) AS customerid,
  COALESCE(fi.transactionid, lg.transactionid) AS transactionid,
  COALESCE(fi.gasstationid, lg.gasstationid) AS gasstationid,
  COALESCE(fi.amount, lg.amount) AS amount
FROM first_issue fi
FULL JOIN last_good lg
  ON lg.customerid = fi.customerid
ORDER BY customerid;

-- Drop the old trigger if it exists to avoid duplicates
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS after_insert_source_error ON public.source_table;

-- Create a proper trigger function that inserts only the NEW row into destination_table
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.trigger_function_error()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $BODY$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.destination_table (
    created_at,
    match_id,
    player_id,
    event_type,
    url,
    user_id
  )
  VALUES (
    NEW.created_at,
    NEW.match_id,
    NEW.player_id,
    NEW.event_type,
    split_part(NEW.url, '?', 1),                 -- simplified URL without query string
    NEW.data #>> '{local_storage,data,user_id}'  -- extract user_id from JSONB
  );

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$BODY$;

-- Recreate the trigger to call the corrected function
CREATE TRIGGER after_insert_source_error
AFTER INSERT ON public.source_table
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION public.trigger_function_error();

WITH search_events AS (
  SELECT
    ua.user_id,
    ua.action_time AS search_time,
    /* last login before this search */
    (
      SELECT MAX(lprev.action_time)
      FROM user_actions AS lprev
      WHERE lprev.user_id = ua.user_id
        AND lprev.action = 'LOGIN'
        AND lprev.action_time < ua.action_time
    ) AS prev_login_time,
    /* next login after this search */
    (
      SELECT MIN(lnext.action_time)
      FROM user_actions AS lnext
      WHERE lnext.user_id = ua.user_id
        AND lnext.action = 'LOGIN'
        AND lnext.action_time > ua.action_time
    ) AS next_login_time
  FROM user_actions AS ua
  WHERE ua.action = 'SEARCH'
),
abandoned_searches AS (
  SELECT se.user_id, se.search_time
  FROM search_events se
  WHERE se.prev_login_time IS NOT NULL           -- ensure the search is after a LOGIN
    AND se.next_login_time IS NOT NULL           -- ensure there is a "next LOGIN" boundary
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM user_actions b
      WHERE b.user_id = se.user_id
        AND b.action = 'BUY'
        AND b.action_time > se.search_time
        AND b.action_time < se.next_login_time   -- no BUY after the SEARCH and before next LOGIN
    )
)
SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) AS abandoned_search_count
FROM abandoned_searches
GROUP BY user_id
ORDER BY user_id;

WITH
  params AS (
    SELECT
      'April Meeting'::text AS event_name,
      'MST'::text AS tz  -- consider 'America/Denver' for DST-aware Mountain Time
  ),
  occ_days AS (
    -- Distinct calendar days (in the requested time zone) on which the event occurred
    SELECT DISTINCT
      (
        ((e.event_date::timestamp) AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')
        AT TIME ZONE (SELECT tz FROM params)
      )::date AS day_in_tz
    FROM event e
    WHERE e.event_name = (SELECT event_name FROM params)
  ),
  ranked AS (
    SELECT
      day_in_tz,
      row_number() OVER (ORDER BY day_in_tz) AS rn
    FROM occ_days
  ),
  grouped AS (
    -- d - rn is constant across consecutive days, creating a "group id" for each streak
    SELECT
      day_in_tz,
      (day_in_tz - rn::int) AS grp
    FROM ranked
  ),
  latest AS (
    -- Most recent day (in the requested time zone) with at least one occurrence
    SELECT max(day_in_tz) AS max_day FROM occ_days
  ),
  target_grp AS (
    -- The group that contains the most recent day is the current streak
    SELECT g.grp
    FROM grouped g
    JOIN latest l ON g.day_in_tz = l.max_day
  )
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS streak_count,
  (SELECT event_name FROM params) AS event_name,
  (SELECT tz FROM params) AS timezone,
  MIN(day_in_tz) AS start_date,
  MAX(day_in_tz) AS end_date
FROM grouped
WHERE grp = (SELECT grp FROM target_grp);

WITH
  qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1
    FROM superhero_group
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
  ),
  qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2
    FROM superhero_group
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
  ),
  combined AS (
    SELECT
      COALESCE(q1.item, q2.item) AS item,
      q1.subgroup AS subgroup1,
      q2.subgroup AS subgroup2
    FROM qr1 q1
    FULL JOIN qr2 q2 ON q1.item = q2.item
  ),
  nodes AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT s AS subgroup
    FROM (
      SELECT subgroup1 AS s FROM combined WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
      UNION ALL
      SELECT subgroup2 AS s FROM combined WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    ) x
  ),
  adj AS (
    -- undirected edges between subgroups that share at least one item
    SELECT subgroup1 AS from_subgroup, subgroup2 AS to_subgroup
    FROM combined
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL AND subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT subgroup2 AS from_subgroup, subgroup1 AS to_subgroup
    FROM combined
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL AND subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
  ),
  -- transitive closure over the undirected graph of subgroups
  reach AS (
    SELECT subgroup AS src, subgroup AS dest
    FROM nodes
    UNION
    SELECT r.src, a.to_subgroup AS dest
    FROM reach r
    JOIN adj a ON a.from_subgroup = r.dest
  ),
  -- assign each subgroup to its component label (minimum reachable subgroup id)
  components AS (
    SELECT src AS subgroup, MIN(dest) AS comp_key
    FROM reach
    GROUP BY src
  ),
  final_map AS (
    SELECT
      c.item,
      c.subgroup1,
      c.subgroup2,
      COALESCE(c1.comp_key, c2.comp_key) AS comp_key
    FROM combined c
    LEFT JOIN components c1 ON c1.subgroup = c.subgroup1
    LEFT JOIN components c2 ON c2.subgroup = c.subgroup2
  )
SELECT
  item,
  subgroup1,
  subgroup2,
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY comp_key) AS group_id
FROM final_map
ORDER BY item;

-- Clean up any previous attempts
DROP AGGREGATE IF EXISTS gapfill(anyelement);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS gapfill_internal(anyelement, anyelement);

-- State transition function: carry forward the last non-null value
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION gapfill_internal(state anyelement, val anyelement)
RETURNS anyelement
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
    IF val IS NULL THEN
        RETURN state;
    ELSE
        RETURN val;
    END IF;
END;
$$;

-- Aggregate to be used as a window function
CREATE AGGREGATE gapfill(anyelement) (
    SFUNC = gapfill_internal,
    STYPE = anyelement
);

DO $$
DECLARE
  cols text;
BEGIN
  -- Build "col = src.col" list for all columns present in BOTH account and trans, except the PK
  SELECT string_agg(format('%I = src.%I', c.column_name, c.column_name), ', ')
  INTO cols
  FROM information_schema.columns c
  WHERE c.table_schema = 'public'
    AND c.table_name = 'account'
    AND c.column_name <> 'account_id'
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM information_schema.columns c2
      WHERE c2.table_schema = 'public'
        AND c2.table_name = 'trans'
        AND c2.column_name = c.column_name
    );

  IF cols IS NULL THEN
    RAISE NOTICE 'No shared columns between account and trans to update.';
    RETURN;
  END IF;

  EXECUTE format($f$
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT DISTINCT ON (account_id) *
      FROM trans
      ORDER BY account_id, trans_id DESC
    )
    UPDATE account AS a
    SET %s
    FROM latest AS src
    WHERE a.account_id = src.account_id
  $f$, cols);
END $$;

-- Recompute eff_to only for the affected team, using the next record's date per team
-- and set last record's eff_to to 5999-12-31.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to()
RETURNS TRIGGER
AS $$
BEGIN
  WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT
      ta.id,
      LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(ta.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::date) OVER (
        PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
        ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(ta.date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), ta.id
      ) AS next_date
    FROM team_attributes ta
    WHERE ta.team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
  )
  UPDATE team_attributes t
  SET eff_to = COALESCE(o.next_date - 1, DATE '5999-12-31')
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE t.id = o.id;

  RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to();

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS update_school_inventory_after_supply() CASCADE;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_school_inventory_after_supply()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $trigger$
BEGIN
    -- Add the newly received quantity to the existing inventory for the same school and supply
    UPDATE school_inventory AS i
    SET
        quantity     = COALESCE(i.quantity, 0) + COALESCE(NEW.quantity, 0),
        last_updated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
        total_value  = CASE
                           WHEN i.unit_value IS NULL THEN NULL
                           ELSE COALESCE(i.unit_value, 0.00) * (COALESCE(i.quantity, 0) + COALESCE(NEW.quantity, 0))
                       END
    WHERE i.cdscode = NEW.cdscode
      AND i.supply_name = NEW.supply_name;

    RETURN NEW;
END;
$trigger$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trigger_insert_supply ON school_supplies;

CREATE TRIGGER trigger_insert_supply
AFTER INSERT ON school_supplies
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION update_school_inventory_after_supply();

WITH RECURSIVE up AS (
  -- Start at each employee's direct boss (level 1)
  SELECT
    e.id    AS employee_id,
    e.salary AS employee_salary,
    m.id    AS boss_id,
    m.salary AS boss_salary,
    1       AS hierarchical_level,
    ARRAY[e.id, m.id]::bigint[] AS path
  FROM staff e
  JOIN staff m ON m.id = e.bossid

  UNION ALL

  -- Walk up the management chain
  SELECT
    up.employee_id,
    up.employee_salary,
    m2.id    AS boss_id,
    m2.salary AS boss_salary,
    up.hierarchical_level + 1 AS hierarchical_level,
    up.path || m2.id
  FROM up
  JOIN staff m1 ON m1.id = up.boss_id
  JOIN staff m2 ON m2.id = m1.bossid
  WHERE m2.id <> ALL (up.path)
),
first_twice AS (
  -- Keep only managers earning at least twice the employee's salary,
  -- and pick the closest one in the chain
  SELECT
    employee_id,
    employee_salary,
    hierarchical_level,
    boss_id,
    boss_salary,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY employee_id ORDER BY hierarchical_level) AS rn
  FROM up
  WHERE boss_salary >= 2 * employee_salary
)
SELECT
  e.id     AS employeeId,
  e.salary AS employeeSalary,
  ft.hierarchical_level,
  ft.boss_id    AS bossId,
  ft.boss_salary AS bossSalary
FROM staff e
LEFT JOIN first_twice ft
  ON ft.employee_id = e.id
 AND ft.rn = 1
ORDER BY e.id;

WITH RECURSIVE
params AS (SELECT 20::numeric AS threshold),
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    t.customer_id,
    t.id,
    t.amount,
    row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY t.customer_id ORDER BY t.id) AS rn
  FROM transfer t
),
cte AS (
  -- Anchor: first row per customer
  SELECT
    o.customer_id,
    o.id,
    o.amount,
    o.amount AS cum_sum,
    1::int AS bucket,
    o.rn
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Recursive: reset starting with the NEXT row if prior cumulative sum reached/exceeded the threshold
  SELECT
    o.customer_id,
    o.id,
    o.amount,
    CASE
      WHEN c.cum_sum >= p.threshold THEN o.amount            -- reset for the next row
      ELSE c.cum_sum + o.amount
    END AS cum_sum,
    CASE
      WHEN c.cum_sum >= p.threshold THEN c.bucket + 1        -- new bucket starts after crossing threshold
      ELSE c.bucket
    END AS bucket,
    o.rn
  FROM cte c
  JOIN ordered o
    ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
   AND o.rn = c.rn + 1
  CROSS JOIN params p
)
SELECT
  customer_id,
  id,
  amount,
  cum_sum,
  bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY customer_id, id;

SELECT
  COALESCE(SUM(amount) FILTER (WHERE "timestamp" = DATE '2022-01-27' - 1), 0) AS yesterday_revenue,
  ROUND(
    (
      COALESCE(SUM(amount) FILTER (WHERE "timestamp" = DATE '2022-01-27' - 1), 0)
      -
      COALESCE(SUM(amount) FILTER (WHERE "timestamp" = DATE '2022-01-27' - 8), 0)
    )
    / NULLIF(SUM(amount) FILTER (WHERE "timestamp" = DATE '2022-01-27' - 8), 0)
  , 2) AS pct_change_week_ago,
  COALESCE(
    SUM(amount) FILTER (
      WHERE "timestamp" >= date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27')::date
        AND "timestamp" <  DATE '2022-01-27'
    ), 0
  ) AS mtd,
  ROUND(
    (
      COALESCE(
        SUM(amount) FILTER (
          WHERE "timestamp" >= date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27')::date
            AND "timestamp" <  DATE '2022-01-27'
        ), 0
      )
      -
      COALESCE(
        SUM(amount) FILTER (
          WHERE "timestamp" >= date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27' - INTERVAL '1 month')::date
            AND "timestamp" <  (date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27' - INTERVAL '1 month')::date
                                + EXTRACT(day FROM (DATE '2022-01-27' - 1))::int)
        ), 0
      )
    )
    / NULLIF(
        SUM(amount) FILTER (
          WHERE "timestamp" >= date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27' - INTERVAL '1 month')::date
            AND "timestamp" <  (date_trunc('month', DATE '2022-01-27' - INTERVAL '1 month')::date
                                + EXTRACT(day FROM (DATE '2022-01-27' - 1))::int)
        ),
        0
      )
  , 2) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM transactions;

-- 1) One row per token (keeps rows with none via LEFT JOIN LATERAL)
--    token = {{...}} (with braces); token_inner = ... (without braces)
SELECT
  c.id,
  c.text,
  t.n AS token_order,
  t.token AS token_inner,
  '{{' || t.token || '}}' AS token
FROM cards AS c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT m[1] AS token, n
  FROM regexp_matches(c.text, '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') WITH ORDINALITY AS r(m, n)
) AS t ON TRUE;


-- 2) One row per card, aggregate tokens into arrays and count them
SELECT
  c.id,
  c.text,
  COALESCE(array_agg('{{' || t.token || '}}' ORDER BY t.n) FILTER (WHERE t.token IS NOT NULL), '{}') AS tokens_with_braces,
  COALESCE(array_agg(t.token ORDER BY t.n) FILTER (WHERE t.token IS NOT NULL), '{}') AS tokens_inner,
  COUNT(t.token) AS token_count
FROM cards AS c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT m[1] AS token, n
  FROM regexp_matches(c.text, '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') WITH ORDINALITY AS r(m, n)
) AS t ON TRUE
GROUP BY c.id, c.text;


-- 3) Optional: tokens as a comma-separated list instead of an array
SELECT
  c.id,
  c.text,
  string_agg('{{' || t.token || '}}', ', ' ORDER BY t.n) AS token_list,
  COUNT(t.token) AS token_count
FROM cards AS c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT m[1] AS token, n
  FROM regexp_matches(c.text, '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') WITH ORDINALITY AS r(m, n)
) AS t ON TRUE
GROUP BY c.id, c.text;

-- Use jsonb_path_exists with a bind variable for the type
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.new_function(p_type text DEFAULT 'Unknown')
RETURNS SETOF bond
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
AS $function$
  SELECT *
  FROM bond
  WHERE jsonb_path_exists(
    bond_stat_,
    '$.**.type ? (@ == $t)',
    jsonb_build_object('t', p_type)
  );
$function$;

-- Query the bond_ids that match (uses default 'Unknown')
SELECT bond_id FROM public.new_function();

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
  SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM schools
  GROUP BY fundingtype
),
calc AS (
  SELECT
    fundingtype,
    cnt,
    cnt * 100.0 / SUM(cnt) OVER () AS exact_pct
  FROM cte_funding_count
),
alloc AS (
  SELECT
    fundingtype,
    cnt,
    CAST(exact_pct AS INT) AS pct_floor,
    exact_pct - CAST(exact_pct AS INT) AS remainder
  FROM calc
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    fundingtype,
    cnt,
    pct_floor,
    remainder,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY remainder DESC, cnt DESC, fundingtype) AS rn,
    100 - SUM(pct_floor) OVER () AS to_distribute
  FROM alloc
)
SELECT
  fundingtype,
  cnt AS "count",
  pct_floor + CASE WHEN rn <= to_distribute THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS percent
FROM ranked
ORDER BY percent DESC, fundingtype;

WITH bond_vals AS (
  SELECT
    b.molecule_id,
    b.bond_id,
    b.bond_type,
    b.bond_stat_ ->> 'type' AS json_type,
    (b.bond_stat_ ->> 'time')::numeric AS time_val
  FROM bond b
  -- ensure the key exists so the ::numeric cast is safe
  WHERE b.bond_stat_ ? 'time'
),
mol_totals AS (
  SELECT
    molecule_id,
    SUM(time_val) AS total_time
  FROM bond_vals
  GROUP BY molecule_id
)
SELECT
  bv.bond_id,
  bv.molecule_id,
  bv.time_val AS bond_time,
  mt.total_time AS molecule_total_time,
  bv.time_val / NULLIF(mt.total_time, 0) AS fraction_of_molecule_total
FROM bond_vals bv
JOIN mol_totals mt USING (molecule_id)
WHERE
  -- treat "Unknown" from either the JSONB 'type' or the column 'bond_type'
  (bv.json_type = 'Unknown' OR bv.bond_type = 'Unknown')
  AND bv.time_val > 0.1 * mt.total_time
ORDER BY
  bv.molecule_id, fraction_of_molecule_total DESC;

    ) AS year_obj
  FROM classyearstudents AS cys

WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    timestamp '2021-01-07 00:00:00' AS start_ts,   -- anchor timepoint
    interval '1 year'               AS duration,   -- total span (e.g., '3 months', '7 days')
    interval '7 days'               AS step        -- bin size (e.g., '1 day', '1 hour')
),
bins AS (
  SELECT
    gs AS bucket_start,
    gs + (SELECT step FROM params) AS bucket_end
  FROM params p,
       LATERAL generate_series(
         p.start_ts,
         p.start_ts + p.duration - p.step,  -- stop so we don't create an empty trailing bin
         p.step
       ) AS gs
)
SELECT
  b.bucket_start AS interval_start,
  COUNT(e."TIMESTAMP") AS sample_count,
  AVG(e."CL") AS cl,
  AVG(e."C") AS c
FROM bins b
LEFT JOIN "Experiment" e
  ON e."TIMESTAMP" >= b.bucket_start
 AND e."TIMESTAMP" <  b.bucket_end
GROUP BY b.bucket_start
ORDER BY b.bucket_start;

WITH reporting_dates AS (

)
SELECT

    WHERE s.opendate <= rd.reporting_date
      AND (s.closeddate IS NULL OR s.closeddate > rd.reporting_date)
  ) AS active_schools
FROM reporting_dates rd
CROSS JOIN schools s
GROUP BY rd.reporting_date
ORDER BY rd.reporting_date;

-- Sum report metrics at the granularity requested by each UTM row,
-- treating NULLs in UTM as wildcards (match all values in report for that dimension).
SELECT
  u.row_id AS id,
  u.utm_campaign,
  u.utm_source,
  u.utm_medium,
  COALESCE(SUM(r.sessions), 0)  AS sessions,
  COALESCE(SUM(r.pageviews), 0) AS pageviews,
  COALESCE(SUM(r.bounces), 0)   AS bounces
FROM utm AS u
LEFT JOIN report AS r
  ON r.id = u.row_id
 AND (u.utm_campaign IS NULL OR r.utm_campaign = u.utm_campaign)
 AND (u.utm_source  IS NULL OR r.utm_source  = u.utm_source)
 AND (u.utm_medium  IS NULL OR r.utm_medium  = u.utm_medium)
-- content and term are intentionally ignored for granularity, so do not restrict the join by them
GROUP BY
  u.row_id, u.utm_campaign, u.utm_source, u.utm_medium
ORDER BY
  u.row_id, u.utm_campaign, u.utm_source, u.utm_medium;

      (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70)
  ),
  -- parameters: n = base group size, k = rows of left context, kp = rows of right context
  params(n, k, kp) AS (VALUES (2, 2, 2)),
  -- assign a position (rn) based on the ordering and compute the base group id (grp)
  pos AS (
    SELECT
      h.*,
      row_number() OVER (ORDER BY hero_id) AS rn,
      (row_number() OVER (ORDER BY hero_id) - 1) / (SELECT n FROM params) AS grp
    FROM hero_attribute h
  ),
  -- boundaries (start/end) of each base group in rn-space
  grp_bounds AS (
    SELECT
      grp,
      MIN(rn) AS rn_start,
      MAX(rn) AS rn_end
    FROM pos
    GROUP BY grp
  ),
  -- total number of rows
  mx AS (SELECT MAX(rn) AS rn_max FROM pos)
SELECT
  SUM(p.attribute_value) AS sum
FROM grp_bounds b
CROSS JOIN mx
JOIN pos p
  ON p.rn BETWEEN GREATEST(b.rn_start - (SELECT k FROM params), 1)
              AND LEAST(b.rn_end   + (SELECT kp FROM params), mx.rn_max)
GROUP BY b.grp
ORDER BY b.grp;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text)
RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text)
LANGUAGE sql
AS $func$
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS out_mine,
    c.msg AS out_msg
  FROM words_chat AS c
  JOIN words_games AS g
    ON g.gid = c.gid
  JOIN words_social AS s
    ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2)
   AND s.social = in_social
   AND s.sid = in_sid
  JOIN words_users AS u_sender
    ON u_sender.uid = c.uid
  WHERE
    c.gid = in_gid
    AND (c.uid = s.uid OR NOT u_sender.muted)
  ORDER BY c.created ASC;
$func$;

WITH
params AS (
  SELECT 500::bigint AS total_budget
),

-- 1) Pick as many Food items as possible within 500 (cheapest first)
food_ranked AS (
  SELECT
    b.budget_id,
    b.category,
    b.amount,
    SUM(b.amount) OVER (
      ORDER BY b.amount, b.budget_id
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS run_amt
  FROM budget b
  WHERE b.category = 'Food'
),
food_picked AS (
  SELECT f.*
  FROM food_ranked f
  WHERE f.run_amt <= (SELECT total_budget FROM params)
),

-- 2) Compute remaining budget after Food
food_spend AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0)::numeric AS spent_food
  FROM food_picked
),

-- 3) With remaining budget, pick as many Advertisement items as possible (cheapest first)
ad_ranked AS (
  SELECT
    b.budget_id,
    b.category,
    b.amount,
    SUM(b.amount) OVER (
      ORDER BY b.amount, b.budget_id
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS run_amt
  FROM budget b
  WHERE b.category = 'Advertisement'
),
ad_picked AS (
  SELECT a.*
  FROM ad_ranked a
  WHERE a.run_amt <= (
    SELECT (p.total_budget - fs.spent_food)
    FROM params p, food_spend fs
  )
)

-- 4) Final selection (Food first, then Advertisements)
SELECT budget_id, category, amount
FROM food_picked
UNION ALL
SELECT budget_id, category, amount
FROM ad_picked
ORDER BY category DESC, amount, budget_id;

    AND avgscrread IS NOT NULL
    AND avgscrmath IS NOT NULL
    AND avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL

  SELECT
    county,
    AVG(school_avg_sat) AS county_avg_sat
  FROM school_scores

  FROM county_avgs

WITH RECURSIVE
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    trx::numeric AS trx,
    event::numeric AS event,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
  FROM sales_transactions
),
rec AS (
  -- Base row
  SELECT
    o.rn,
    o.id,
    o.trx,
    o.event,
    o.trx AS sum_trx,
    ROUND(o.event * o.trx, 6)::numeric(20,6) AS result,
    ROUND(o.event * o.trx, 6)::numeric(20,6) AS cum_result
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Recursive step
  SELECT
    o.rn,
    o.id,
    o.trx,
    o.event,
    r.sum_trx + o.trx AS sum_trx,
    ROUND(o.event * (r.sum_trx + o.trx + r.cum_result), 6)::numeric(20,6) AS result,
    (r.cum_result + ROUND(o.event * (r.sum_trx + o.trx + r.cum_result), 6))::numeric(20,6) AS cum_result
  FROM rec r
  JOIN ordered o
    ON o.rn = r.rn + 1
)
SELECT id, result
FROM rec
ORDER BY id;

-- Make the dependencies follow stage changes automatically

-- 1) Ensure dependency FKs reference (id, stage) with ON UPDATE CASCADE
ALTER TABLE "dependency"
  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match1_stage1_fkey,
  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match2_stage2_fkey;

ALTER TABLE "dependency"
  ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match1_stage1_fkey
    FOREIGN KEY (match1, stage1)
    REFERENCES "match"(id, stage)
    ON UPDATE CASCADE,
  ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match2_stage2_fkey
    FOREIGN KEY (match2, stage2)
    REFERENCES "match"(id, stage)
    ON UPDATE CASCADE;

-- 2) Now you can safely bump the stage; dependencies will update automatically
UPDATE "match"
SET stage = stage + 1
WHERE id = 'match1';

-- Find which character varying column(s) your row would overflow
WITH new_row AS (
  SELECT jsonb_build_object(
    'Age', 18,
    'Gender', 'Male',
    'Geographic_Location', 'Urban',
    'Socioeconomic_Background', 'Low',
    'Education_Level', 'UndergraduateUndergraduateUndergraduate',
    'Previous_Academic_Performance', 88.55,
    'Language_Proficiency', 'Intermediate',
    'Preferred_Learning_Medium', 'Video',
    'Learning_Pace', 'Moderate',
    'Engagement_History', 6
  ) AS j
)
SELECT
  c.column_name,
  c.character_maximum_length AS max_len,
  char_length(nr.j ->> c.column_name) AS provided_len,
  nr.j ->> c.column_name AS provided_value
FROM information_schema.columns c
CROSS JOIN new_row nr
WHERE c.table_schema = 'public'
  AND c.table_name = 'learners'
  AND c.data_type IN ('character varying', 'character')
  AND c.character_maximum_length IS NOT NULL
  AND nr.j ? c.column_name
  AND char_length(nr.j ->> c.column_name) > c.character_maximum_length;

WITH day_start AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (card_id, date_value::date)
         card_id,
         date_value::date AS trading_day,
         date_value AS start_ts,
         price AS start_price
  FROM card_prices
  ORDER BY card_id, date_value::date, date_value ASC, id ASC
),
day_end AS (
  SELECT
    ds.card_id,
    ds.trading_day,
    ds.start_ts,
    ds.start_price,
    e.end_ts,
    e.end_price
  FROM day_start ds
  JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT
      cp.date_value AS end_ts,
      cp.price AS end_price
    FROM card_prices cp
    WHERE cp.card_id = ds.card_id
      AND cp.date_value::date = ds.trading_day
      AND cp.date_value <= ds.start_ts + interval '15 minutes'
    ORDER BY cp.date_value DESC, cp.id DESC
    LIMIT 1
  ) e ON TRUE
),
diffs AS (
  SELECT
    card_id,
    trading_day,
    (end_price - start_price) AS difference
  FROM day_end
)
SELECT
  card_id,
  AVG(difference) AS avg_difference
FROM diffs
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id;

-- Preview (SELECT): returns data with non-tcgplayer/cardkingdom URLs md5-hashed
SELECT
  t.id,
  jsonb_set(
    t.data,
    '{purchaseurls}',
    COALESCE(
      (
        SELECT jsonb_agg(
          CASE
            WHEN COALESCE(substring(url FROM '://([^/:?#]+)'), '') ~* '(^|\.)tcgplayer\.com$'
              OR COALESCE(substring(url FROM '://([^/:?#]+)'), '') ~* '(^|\.)cardkingdom\.com$'
            THEN to_jsonb(url)              -- keep as-is
            ELSE to_jsonb(md5(url))         -- hash other domains
          END
        )
        FROM jsonb_array_elements_text(COALESCE(t.data->'purchaseurls', '[]'::jsonb)) AS u(url)
      ),
      '[]'::jsonb
    ),
    true
  ) AS data_sanitized
FROM cards_info t;

-- Persist (UPDATE): updates the JSONB in-place
UPDATE cards_info t
SET data = jsonb_set(
  t.data,
  '{purchaseurls}',
  s.new_purchaseurls,
  true
)
FROM (
  SELECT
    c.id,
    jsonb_agg(
      CASE
        WHEN COALESCE(substring(url FROM '://([^/:?#]+)'), '') ~* '(^|\.)tcgplayer\.com$'
          OR COALESCE(substring(url FROM '://([^/:?#]+)'), '') ~* '(^|\.)cardkingdom\.com$'
        THEN to_jsonb(url)
        ELSE to_jsonb(md5(url))
      END
    ) AS new_purchaseurls
  FROM cards_info c
  CROSS JOIN jsonb_array_elements_text(COALESCE(c.data->'purchaseurls', '[]'::jsonb)) AS u(url)
  WHERE jsonb_typeof(COALESCE(c.data->'purchaseurls', '[]'::jsonb)) = 'array'
  GROUP BY c.id
) s
WHERE t.id = s.id;

WITH pt AS (
  SELECT vv.id, vv.name
  FROM variablevalue vv
  JOIN variable v ON v.id = vv.variable_id
  WHERE v.name = 'ProductType'
),
lang AS (
  SELECT vv.id, vv.name
  FROM variablevalue vv
  JOIN variable v ON v.id = vv.variable_id
  WHERE v.name = 'publishing_language'
),
genre AS (
  SELECT vv.id, vv.name
  FROM variablevalue vv
  JOIN variable v ON v.id = vv.variable_id
  WHERE v.name = 'genre'
)
SELECT
  td.years AS year,
  sd.name AS country,
  pt.name AS product_type,
  lang.name AS language,
  genre.name AS genre,
  SUM(
    CASE
      WHEN dp.id IS NOT NULL
        AND EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
          WHERE dv.datapoint_id = dp.id
            AND dv.variablevalue_id = pt.id
        )
        AND EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
          WHERE dv.datapoint_id = dp.id
            AND dv.variablevalue_id = lang.id
        )
        AND EXISTS (
          SELECT 1 FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
          WHERE dv.datapoint_id = dp.id
            AND dv.variablevalue_id = genre.id
        )
      THEN dp.value
      ELSE 0
    END
  ) AS total_sales
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN pt
CROSS JOIN lang
CROSS JOIN genre
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp
  ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id
 AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, pt.name, lang.name, genre.name
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, pt.name, lang.name, genre.name;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    LEAST(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS start_ts,
    GREATEST(TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24') AS end_ts
),
days AS (
  SELECT
    d::timestamp AS day_start,
    (d + interval '1 day') AS day_end
  FROM params p
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
    date_trunc('day', p.start_ts),
    date_trunc('day', p.end_ts),
    interval '1 day'
  ) AS d
  WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM d) BETWEEN 1 AND 5  -- weekdays only (Mon=1 .. Fri=5)
),
overlaps AS (
  SELECT
    GREATEST(p.start_ts, day_start) AS seg_start,
    LEAST(p.end_ts, day_end) AS seg_end
  FROM days
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
sum_seconds AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM seg_end - seg_start)), 0) AS total_seconds
  FROM overlaps
  WHERE seg_end > seg_start
),
parts AS (
  SELECT
    FLOOR(total_seconds / 86400)::bigint AS days,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 86400) / 3600)::bigint AS hours,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 3600) / 60)::bigint AS minutes,
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 60))::bigint AS seconds
  FROM sum_seconds
)
SELECT
  days || ' days ' || hours || ' hours ' || minutes || ' minutes ' || seconds || ' seconds' AS time_diff
FROM parts;

WITH
-- 1) One row per item with a clean retail price (ignore NaN/infinite)
items AS (
  SELECT
    s.item_id,
    MIN(CASE WHEN isfinite(s.item_price) THEN s.item_price END) AS retail_price
  FROM store s
  GROUP BY s.item_id
),

-- 2) Direct resource-only craft cost (partial craft price)
partial AS (
  SELECT
    s.item_id,
    COALESCE(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND isfinite(s.resource_price)
            THEN s.resource_price * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0)
        END
      ),
      0
    ) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM store s
  GROUP BY s.item_id
),

-- 3) Expand the bill-of-materials recursively over item dependencies
--    mult = product of quantities along the dependency path
bom AS (
  SELECT
    i.item_id AS root_item,
    i.item_id AS current_item,
    1::numeric AS mult,
    ARRAY[i.item_id]::int[] AS path
  FROM items i

  UNION ALL

  SELECT
    b.root_item,
    s.uses_item_id AS current_item,
    b.mult * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0),
    b.path || s.uses_item_id
  FROM bom b
  JOIN store s
    ON s.item_id = b.current_item
  WHERE s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
    -- avoid cycles if any exist
    AND NOT (s.uses_item_id = ANY (b.path))
),

-- 4) Full craft price = sum of all resources needed across the entire dependency tree
full_cost AS (
  SELECT
    b.root_item AS item_id,
    COALESCE(
      SUM(
        CASE
          WHEN s.resource_id IS NOT NULL AND isfinite(s.resource_price)
            THEN s.resource_price * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0) * b.mult
        END
      ),
      0
    ) AS full_craft_price
  FROM bom b
  JOIN store s
    ON s.item_id = b.current_item
  GROUP BY b.root_item
)

SELECT
  i.item_id,
  i.retail_price,
  p.partial_craft_price,
  f.full_craft_price,
  -- Optional: which is cheaper overall (buy vs craft)
  CASE
    WHEN i.retail_price IS NULL THEN 'CRAFT'
    WHEN f.full_craft_price IS NULL THEN 'BUY'
    WHEN f.full_craft_price < i.retail_price THEN 'CRAFT'
    ELSE 'BUY'
  END AS cheaper_option
FROM items i
LEFT JOIN partial p ON p.item_id = i.item_id
LEFT JOIN full_cost f ON f.item_id = i.item_id
ORDER BY i.item_id;

WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT
    inst."Institution_Type",
    outcm."Pre_Test_Score",
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (
      PARTITION BY inst."Institution_Type"
      ORDER BY outcm."Pre_Test_Score" DESC
    ) AS score_rank
  FROM outcomes AS outcm
  JOIN enrollment AS enrl
    ON outcm.learner_code = enrl.learner_code
  JOIN institutions AS inst
    ON enrl.institution_key = inst.institution_key
  WHERE inst."Institution_Type" IN ('Public', 'Private')
)
SELECT
  -- Averages by institution type
  AVG(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Public' THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END)  AS avg_public_pre_test,
  AVG(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Private' THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END) AS avg_private_pre_test,
  AVG(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Public' THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END)
    - AVG(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Private' THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END) AS avg_score_diff,

  -- Top (max) pre-test score by institution type (equivalent to dense_rank = 1)
  MAX(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Public'  AND score_rank = 1 THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END) AS top_public_pre_test,
  MAX(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Private' AND score_rank = 1 THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END) AS top_private_pre_test,
  MAX(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Public'  AND score_rank = 1 THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END)
    - MAX(CASE WHEN "Institution_Type" = 'Private' AND score_rank = 1 THEN "Pre_Test_Score" END) AS top_score_diff
FROM ranked;

WITH buys AS (
  SELECT
    t.molecule_id,
    t.date AS buy_date,
    t.transaction_id AS buy_txn_id,
    t.molecules AS buy_qty,
    t.usd_total AS buy_total,
    (t.usd_total / t.molecules::numeric) AS buy_unit_price,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) - t.molecules AS buy_cum_start,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) AS buy_cum_end
  FROM "transaction" t
  WHERE t.sell = false
),
sells AS (
  SELECT
    t.molecule_id,
    t.date AS sell_date,
    t.transaction_id AS sell_txn_id,
    t.molecules AS sell_qty,
    t.usd_total AS sell_total,
    (t.usd_total / t.molecules::numeric) AS sell_unit_price,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) - t.molecules AS sell_cum_start,
    SUM(t.molecules) OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.molecule_id
      ORDER BY t.date, t.transaction_id
    ) AS sell_cum_end
  FROM "transaction" t
  WHERE t.sell = true
),
matches AS (
  SELECT
    b.molecule_id,
    b.buy_date,
    s.sell_date,
    GREATEST(
      0,
      LEAST(b.buy_cum_end, s.sell_cum_end) - GREATEST(b.buy_cum_start, s.sell_cum_start)
    )::integer AS matched_qty,
    b.buy_unit_price,
    s.sell_unit_price
  FROM buys b
  JOIN sells s
    ON s.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
   AND (
         s.sell_date > b.buy_date
      OR (s.sell_date = b.buy_date AND s.sell_txn_id > b.buy_txn_id)
   )
  WHERE LEAST(b.buy_cum_end, s.sell_cum_end) > GREATEST(b.buy_cum_start, s.sell_cum_start)
)
SELECT
  molecule_id,
  buy_date,
  sell_date,
  matched_qty AS molecules,
  (matched_qty * buy_unit_price) AS buy_usd_total,
  (matched_qty * sell_unit_price) AS sell_usd_total
FROM matches
WHERE matched_qty > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, buy_date, sell_date;

WITH t AS (
  SELECT id, string_to_array(event_description, '#') AS a
  FROM match_events
),
min_len AS (
  SELECT MIN(array_length(a, 1)) AS min_len
  FROM t
),
cnts AS (
  SELECT pos, COUNT(DISTINCT a[pos]) AS cnt
  FROM t
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_subscripts(a, 1) AS pos
  WHERE pos <= (SELECT min_len FROM min_len)
  GROUP BY pos
),
prefix_len AS (
  SELECT COALESCE(
           (SELECT MIN(pos) - 1 FROM cnts WHERE cnt > 1),
           (SELECT min_len FROM min_len)
         ) AS len
),
any_row AS (
  SELECT a
  FROM t
  ORDER BY id
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN (SELECT len FROM prefix_len) > 0
      THEN array_to_string((SELECT a[1:(SELECT len FROM prefix_len)] FROM any_row), '#')
    ELSE ''
  END AS common_prefix;

SELECT
  begin_date,
  end_date,
  CASE
    WHEN start_month > end_month THEN 0
    ELSE (
      (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM end_month)::int * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM end_month)::int)
      - (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM start_month)::int * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM start_month)::int)
      + 1
    )
  END AS full_calendar_months
FROM (
  SELECT
    begin_date,
    end_date,
    -- first full month to count (include start month only if it starts on the 1st)
    (date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
       + CASE WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
              THEN interval '0 month'
              ELSE interval '1 month'
         END
    )::date AS start_month,
    -- last full month to count (exclude the month containing end_date)
    (date_trunc('month', end_date)::date - interval '1 month')::date AS end_month
  FROM race_dates
) m;

-- Find institutions within 1 meter of the target learner's institution location
-- (ST_Touches won't work for point geometries; use ST_DWithin instead. Casting to geography makes distance meters.)
SELECT

  ol.institution_name,
  ST_Distance(ol.geom::geography, l.geom::geography) AS distance_m
FROM institution_location AS ol
JOIN learner_institution_location AS l
  ON ST_DWithin(ol.geom::geography, l.geom::geography, 1)  -- 1 meter; adjust as needed
WHERE l.learner_id = $1                                    -- supply the target learner_id
  AND ol.institution_id <> l.institution_id
ORDER BY distance_m;

-- Distinct values for one key ('dis') per customer
SELECT
  customerid,
  COALESCE(
    jsonb_agg(DISTINCT to_jsonb(v) ORDER BY v) FILTER (WHERE v IS NOT NULL),
    '[]'::jsonb
  ) AS dis_values
FROM card_preference
CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements_text(COALESCE(preference->'dis', '[]'::jsonb)) AS a(v)
WHERE customerid = '123.abc'
GROUP BY customerid;

-- Distinct values for ALL top-level keys per customer (each key -> array of distinct values)
WITH kv AS (
  SELECT cp.customerid, e.key, e.value
  FROM card_preference cp
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_each(cp.preference) AS e(key, value)
),
flatten AS (
  SELECT
    k.customerid,
    k.key,
    CASE WHEN jsonb_typeof(k.value) = 'array' THEN elem ELSE k.value END AS val
  FROM kv k
  LEFT JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(k.value) AS arr(elem)
    ON jsonb_typeof(k.value) = 'array'
),
per_key AS (
  SELECT customerid, key, jsonb_agg(DISTINCT val) AS vals
  FROM flatten
  GROUP BY customerid, key
)
SELECT customerid, jsonb_object_agg(key, vals) AS preferences_union
FROM per_key
GROUP BY customerid;

-- Create a robust name-concatenation function
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION fn_concat(f_name character varying, l_name character varying)
RETURNS character varying
LANGUAGE SQL
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
  SELECT NULLIF(BTRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', $1, $2)), '')
$$;

-- Use the function to get the requested output
SELECT
  c.customer_id,
  c.f_name,
  c.l_name,
  c.email AS email_address,

FROM cust AS c;

SELECT
  p.uuid        AS parent_uuid,
  p.card_name   AS parent_name,
  COALESCE(
    array_agg(c.uuid) FILTER (WHERE c.uuid IS NOT NULL),
    ARRAY[]::uuid[]

WITH RECURSIVE neighbors AS (
  -- Users are neighbors if they share the same ref1 or the same ref2
  SELECT u1.id AS src, u2.id AS dst
  FROM "users" u1
  JOIN "users" u2
    ON (u1.ref1 = u2.ref1 OR u1.ref2 = u2.ref2)
   AND u1.id <> u2.id
),
walk AS (
  -- Start with each user as its own root
  SELECT u.id AS node, u.id AS root
  FROM "users" u
  UNION ALL
  -- Iteratively walk to smaller-id neighbors to find the minimal id in each component
  SELECT w.node, LEAST(w.root, n.dst) AS root
  FROM walk w
  JOIN neighbors n
    ON n.src = w.root
   AND n.dst < w.root
),
components AS (
  -- Each node (user) gets the minimal id reachable as its component/group id
  SELECT node, MIN(root) AS group_id
  FROM walk
  GROUP BY node
),
grouped_users AS (
  -- Aggregate user info per connected component
  SELECT
    c.group_id,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.id) AS ids,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.username) AS usernames,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref1) AS refs1,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT u.ref2) AS refs2
  FROM components c
  JOIN "users" u ON u.id = c.node
  GROUP BY c.group_id
),
matched AS (
  -- Assets whose owner matches any ref in a known user component
  SELECT
    gu.ids,
    gu.usernames,
    gu.refs1,
    gu.refs2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_ids,
    MIN(a.id) AS min_asset_id
  FROM grouped_users gu
  JOIN "users" u ON u.id = ANY(gu.ids)
  JOIN "assets" a ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
  GROUP BY gu.ids, gu.usernames, gu.refs1, gu.refs2
),
unmatched AS (
  -- Assets whose owner doesn't exist in users: propagate owner into refs2
  SELECT
    ARRAY[]::integer[] AS ids,
    ARRAY[]::text[] AS usernames,
    ARRAY[]::text[] AS refs1,
    ARRAY[a.owner] AS refs2,
    COUNT(*) AS asset_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(a.id ORDER BY a.id) AS asset_ids,
    MIN(a.id) AS min_asset_id
  FROM "assets" a
  LEFT JOIN "users" u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
  WHERE u.id IS NULL
  GROUP BY a.owner
)
SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids
FROM (
  SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids, min_asset_id
  FROM matched
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids, min_asset_id
  FROM unmatched
) x
ORDER BY min_asset_id;

DO $$
DECLARE
    r record;
BEGIN
    FOR r IN
        SELECT c.table_schema, c.table_name, c.column_name
        FROM information_schema.columns c
        JOIN information_schema.tables t
          ON c.table_schema = t.table_schema
         AND c.table_name   = t.table_name
        WHERE c.table_schema NOT IN ('pg_catalog', 'information_schema')
          AND t.table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
          AND c.column_name <> lower(c.column_name)
        ORDER BY c.table_schema, c.table_name, c.ordinal_position
    LOOP
        -- Skip if the target lowercase column name already exists in the table
        IF NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM information_schema.columns ic
            WHERE ic.table_schema = r.table_schema
              AND ic.table_name   = r.table_name
              AND ic.column_name  = lower(r.column_name)
        ) THEN
            EXECUTE format(
                'ALTER TABLE %I.%I RENAME COLUMN %I TO %I',
                r.table_schema, r.table_name, r.column_name, lower(r.column_name)
            );
        END IF;
    END LOOP;
END
$$;

WITH month_bounds AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE)::date AS month_start,
    (date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')::date AS month_end
),
first_sunday AS (
  SELECT
    month_start + (7 - EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM month_start)::int) AS first_sunday
  FROM month_bounds
),
week_ranges AS (
  -- First (possibly partial) week: from the 1st of the month to the first Sunday
  SELECT
    1 AS week_no,
    mb.month_start AS week_start,
    LEAST(mb.month_end, fs.first_sunday) AS week_end
  FROM month_bounds mb
  CROSS JOIN first_sunday fs

  UNION ALL

  -- Subsequent weeks: Monday..Sunday, capped at month_end
  SELECT
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ws) + 1 AS week_no,
    ws::date AS week_start,
    LEAST(ws::date + 6, mb.month_end) AS week_end
  FROM month_bounds mb
  CROSS JOIN first_sunday fs
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
      (fs.first_sunday + 1)::timestamp,
      mb.month_end::timestamp,
      INTERVAL '7 days'
  ) AS ws
)
SELECT
  w.week_no,
  w.week_start,
  w.week_end,
  COUNT(l.id) AS record_count
FROM week_ranges w
LEFT JOIN laboratory l
  ON l."date" >= w.week_start
 AND l."date" <= w.week_end
GROUP BY w.week_no, w.week_start, w.week_end
ORDER BY w.week_no;

ALTER TABLE curricula

ALTER TABLE educational_resources
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';

-- Set the target curriculum here
-- You can change 1 to the curriculum_id for "Introduction to Programming"
WITH RECURSIVE
params AS (
  SELECT 1::int AS root_curriculum_id
),
-- Traverse the bipartite graph: curriculum <-> resource via curriculum_resources
dep_chain AS (
  -- Seed: start from the chosen curriculum
  SELECT
      'curriculum'::text AS node_type,
      p.root_curriculum_id::bigint AS node_id,
      ARRAY['curriculum:' || p.root_curriculum_id::text]::text[] AS visited
  FROM params p

  UNION ALL

  -- Step from curriculum -> resources it uses
  SELECT
      'resource',
      cr.resource_key::bigint,
      dc.visited || ('resource:' || cr.resource_key)::text
  FROM dep_chain dc
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr
    ON dc.node_type = 'curriculum'
   AND cr.curriculum_id = dc.node_id
  WHERE NOT (('resource:' || cr.resource_key)::text = ANY(dc.visited))

  UNION ALL

  -- Step from resource -> curricula that use that resource
  SELECT
      'curriculum',
      cr.curriculum_id::bigint,
      dc.visited || ('curriculum:' || cr.curriculum_id)::text
  FROM dep_chain dc
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr
    ON dc.node_type = 'resource'
   AND cr.resource_key = dc.node_id
  WHERE NOT (('curriculum:' || cr.curriculum_id)::text = ANY(dc.visited))
),
to_upgrade_curricula AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT node_id::int AS curriculum_id
  FROM dep_chain
  WHERE node_type = 'curriculum'
),
to_upgrade_resources AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT node_id::int AS resource_key
  FROM dep_chain
  WHERE node_type = 'resource'
)

-- Update all curricula in the transitive dependency chain
UPDATE curricula c
SET status = 'to upgrade'
FROM to_upgrade_curricula t
WHERE c.curriculum_id = t.curriculum_id
  AND c.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'to upgrade';

-- Optionally, also flag all involved resources (since they depend on the curricula)
UPDATE educational_resources er
SET status = 'to upgrade'
FROM to_upgrade_resources t
WHERE er.resource_key = t.resource_key
  AND er.status IS DISTINCT FROM 'to upgrade';

  SELECT
    b.link_to_event,
    bool_or(b.event_status = 'Closed') AS has_closed,
    bool_or(b.event_status = 'Open') AS has_open,

    MAX(CASE WHEN b.event_status = 'Closed' THEN b.remaining END) AS max_remaining_closed,
    MAX(CASE WHEN b.event_status = 'Open' THEN b.spent END) AS max_spent_open,
    MAX(CASE WHEN b.event_status = 'Planning' THEN b.spent END) AS max_spent_planning

    WHEN s.has_open AND s.has_planning THEN 'Open'
    WHEN s.has_open THEN 'Open'
    WHEN s.has_planning THEN 'Planning'
    ELSE 'Unknown'
  END AS consolidated_status,
  CASE
    WHEN s.has_closed THEN s.max_remaining_closed
    WHEN s.has_open AND s.has_planning THEN COALESCE(s.max_spent_open, 0) + COALESCE(s.max_spent_planning, 0)
    WHEN s.has_open THEN s.max_spent_open

  r.cluster AS right_id,
  r.cluster_type AS right_type,
  r.cluster_label AS right_label,
  COUNT(DISTINCT l.article) AS articles,
  la.link_types
FROM tag AS l

  ON l.article = r.article
 AND l.cluster < r.cluster
LEFT JOIN links_agg AS la
  ON l.cluster = la.smol
 AND r.cluster = la.big
GROUP BY
  l.cluster, l.cluster_type, l.cluster_label,
  r.cluster, r.cluster_type, r.cluster_label,
  la.link_types
ORDER BY articles DESC;

-- Collapse contiguous/overlapping ranges per (alb, art, feat_arts)
-- and merge them by updating the first row of each range and deleting the rest.
-- NOTE: Change "sp_album" to your actual table name if needed.

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    a.ctid,
    a.alb,
    a.art,
    a.feat_arts,
    a.start_date,
    a.end_date,
    lag(a.end_date) OVER (
      PARTITION BY a.alb, a.art, a.feat_arts
      ORDER BY a.start_date, a.end_date NULLS LAST
    ) AS prev_end
  FROM sp_album AS a
),
grps AS (
  SELECT
    o.*,
    -- Start a new group when not contiguous/overlapping with the previous row
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN prev_end IS NOT NULL AND o.start_date <= prev_end + 1 THEN 0
        ELSE 1
      END
    ) OVER (
      PARTITION BY o.alb, o.art, o.feat_arts
      ORDER BY o.start_date, o.end_date NULLS LAST
      ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
    ) AS grp
  FROM ordered AS o
),
collapsed AS (
  SELECT
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    grp,
    MIN(start_date) AS start_date,               -- earliest start of the group
    CASE
      WHEN BOOL_OR(end_date IS NULL) THEN NULL   -- if any row is open-ended, result is open-ended
      ELSE MAX(end_date)
    END AS end_date
  FROM grps
  GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
),
keepers AS (
  -- Pick the earliest row in each group to keep and update
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (alb, art, feat_arts, grp)
    ctid AS keep_ctid,
    alb, art, feat_arts, grp
  FROM grps
  ORDER BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp, start_date, end_date NULLS LAST
),
upd AS (
  UPDATE sp_album a
  SET end_date = c.end_date
  FROM keepers k
  JOIN collapsed c USING (alb, art, feat_arts, grp)
  WHERE a.ctid = k.keep_ctid
  RETURNING a.ctid
),
del AS (
  DELETE FROM sp_album a
  USING grps g
  JOIN keepers k USING (alb, art, feat_arts, grp)
  WHERE a.ctid = g.ctid
    AND g.ctid <> k.keep_ctid
  RETURNING a.ctid
)
SELECT
  (SELECT count(*) FROM upd) AS rows_updated,
  (SELECT count(*) FROM del) AS rows_deleted;

SELECT

  a2.element AS element2
FROM molecule AS m
JOIN atom AS a1
  ON a1.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
JOIN connected AS c
  ON c.atom_id = a1.atom_id
JOIN bond AS b
  ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
 AND b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
JOIN atom AS a2
  ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
 AND a2.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
ORDER BY
  m.molecule_id,

FROM seq
GROUP BY grp

SELECT

  m.label AS molecule_label,
  a1.atom_id  AS atom1_id,
  a1.element  AS atom1_element,
  a2.atom_id  AS atom2_id,
  a2.element  AS atom2_element,
  b.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM bond b
JOIN molecule  m ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
JOIN connected c ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
JOIN atom a1     ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id  AND a1.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
JOIN atom a2     ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2 AND a2.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
-- keep each connection once (connected typically has both directions)
WHERE a1.atom_id < a2.atom_id
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, a1.atom_id, a2.atom_id, b.bond_id;

WITH ordered AS (

    r.*,
    LEAD(r.valid_from) OVER (ORDER BY r.valid_from) AS next_valid_from
  FROM rules r
),
periods AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    -- Build the effective period as [valid_from, effective_end)
    -- effective_end = (valid_to + 1 day) if valid_to exists,
    --               = next_valid_from if the next rate starts,
    --               = unbounded if neither exists (still ongoing)
    daterange(valid_from, COALESCE(valid_to + 1, next_valid_from), '[)') AS effective_period
  FROM ordered
)

WITH changes AS (
  SELECT start AS t, 1 AS delta
  FROM duration
  WHERE start IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT "end" AS t, -1 AS delta
  FROM duration
  WHERE "end" IS NOT NULL
),
points AS (
  SELECT t, SUM(delta) AS net_delta
  FROM changes
  GROUP BY t
),
timeline AS (
  SELECT
    t AS t_from,
    LEAD(t) OVER (ORDER BY t) AS t_to,
    SUM(net_delta) OVER (ORDER BY t ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS concurrent_count
  FROM points
)
SELECT t_from, t_to, concurrent_count
FROM timeline
WHERE t_to IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY t_from;

)

  max(element) FILTER (WHERE rn = 1) AS element1,
  max(element) FILTER (WHERE rn = 2) AS element2,
  max(element) FILTER (WHERE rn = 3) AS element3
FROM atomrns
WHERE rn <= 3
GROUP BY molecule_id
ORDER BY molecule_id;

LEFT JOIN team_attributes AS ta
  ON t.team_api_id = ta.team_api_id
 AND ta.date = '2013-09-20 00:00:00'

WITH ranked AS (
  SELECT
    c."atom_id",
    c."bond_id",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c."atom_id" ORDER BY c."bond_id") AS rn
  FROM "connected" c
)
SELECT
  a."atom_id",
  MAX(CASE WHEN r.rn = 1 THEN r."bond_id" END) AS "bond_id_1",
  MAX(CASE WHEN r.rn = 2 THEN r."bond_id" END) AS "bond_id_2",
  MAX(CASE WHEN r.rn = 3 THEN r."bond_id" END) AS "bond_id_3"
FROM "atom" a
LEFT JOIN ranked r
  ON r."atom_id" = a."atom_id" AND r.rn <= 3
GROUP BY a."atom_id"
ORDER BY a."atom_id";

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    "date" AS current_date,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY "date") AS rn,
    LAG("date") OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY "date") AS prev_date
  FROM laboratory
  WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  id AS patient_id,
  prev_date AS previous_test_date,
  rn - 1 AS previous_seq,
  current_date AS current_test_date,
  rn AS current_seq,
  (current_date - prev_date) AS days_between
FROM ordered
WHERE prev_date IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY id, current_date;

SELECT
  m.molecule_id,
  m.label,
  c.atom_id  AS atom1_id,
  a1.element AS atom1_element,
  c.atom_id2 AS atom2_id,
  a2.element AS atom2_element,
  b.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM connected AS c
JOIN atom AS a1 ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
JOIN atom AS a2 ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
JOIN bond AS b ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
JOIN molecule AS m ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id
  AND a1.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
  AND c.atom_id < c.atom_id2
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, atom1_id, atom2_id;

WITH global_avgs AS (
  SELECT
    AVG(buildupplayspeed::numeric) AS avg_speed,
    AVG(defenceteamwidth::numeric) AS avg_width
  FROM team_attributes
)
SELECT
  ta.id,
  ta.team_api_id,
  ta.date,
  ta.buildupplayspeed,
  ta.buildupplaypassing,
  ta.chancecreationpassing,
  ta.chancecreationcrossing,
  ta.chancecreationshooting,
  ta.defencepressure,
  ta.defenceaggression,
  ta.defenceteamwidth
FROM team_attributes AS ta
CROSS JOIN global_avgs AS g
WHERE ta.buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL
  AND ta.defenceteamwidth IS NOT NULL
  AND ta.buildupplayspeed > g.avg_speed
  AND ta.defenceteamwidth < g.avg_width
ORDER BY ta.buildupplayspeed DESC NULLS LAST,
         ta.defenceteamwidth ASC NULLS FIRST

SELECT s."cds",
       s."sname"::text,
       (
         SELECT json_agg(
                  json_build_object(
                    'enroll12', t."enroll12"::text,
                    'NumTstTakr', t."numtsttakr"::text,
                    'AvgScrRead', t."avgscrread"::text,
                    'AvgScrMath', t."avgscrmath"::text,
                    'AvgScrWrite', t."avgscrwrite"::text
                  )
                  ORDER BY t."enroll12" DESC
                )
         FROM "satscores" AS t
         WHERE t."rtype" = 'S'
           AND t."cds" = s."cds"
       ) AS sat_summary
FROM "satscores" AS s
WHERE s."rtype" = 'S';

-- Simple and correct when the exclusion list has no NULLs
SELECT *
FROM molecule
WHERE molecule_id NOT IN ('TR000', 'TR001', 'TR002');

-- Robust pattern when the exclusion list might contain NULLs
WITH ex(id) AS (
  VALUES ('TR000'), ('TR001'), ('TR002'), (NULL)
)
SELECT m.*
FROM molecule AS m
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM ex
  WHERE ex.id = m.molecule_id
);

WITH sequenced AS (
  SELECT
    ta.id,
    ta.team_api_id,
    ta.date::timestamp AS record_ts,
    ta.buildupplayspeed,
    ta.buildupplaypassing,
    ta.defenceteamwidth,
    ta.chancecreationshooting,
    LEAD(ta.buildupplayspeed) OVER (
      PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
      ORDER BY ta.date::timestamp NULLS LAST, ta.id
    ) AS next_buildupplayspeed,
    LEAD(ta.buildupplaypassing) OVER (
      PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
      ORDER BY ta.date::timestamp NULLS LAST, ta.id
    ) AS next_buildupplaypassing,
    LAG(ta.defenceteamwidth) OVER (
      PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
      ORDER BY ta.date::timestamp NULLS LAST, ta.id
    ) AS prev_defenceteamwidth,
    LAG(ta.chancecreationshooting) OVER (
      PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id
      ORDER BY ta.date::timestamp NULLS LAST, ta.id
    ) AS prev_chancecreationshooting,
    COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY ta.team_api_id) AS team_record_count
  FROM team_attributes AS ta
)
SELECT
  id,
  team_api_id,
  record_ts,
  buildupplayspeed,
  next_buildupplayspeed,
  buildupplaypassing,
  next_buildupplaypassing,
  defenceteamwidth,
  prev_defenceteamwidth,
  chancecreationshooting,
  prev_chancecreationshooting,
  team_record_count
FROM sequenced
WHERE team_record_count > 1
  AND prev_defenceteamwidth > buildupplayspeed
ORDER BY team_api_id, record_ts;

WITH counts AS (
  SELECT
    name,
    COUNT(*) AS prints
  FROM cards
  WHERE name IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY name
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    name,
    prints,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prints DESC, name) AS rn
  FROM counts
)
SELECT name, prints
FROM ranked
WHERE rn <= 3
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Others' AS name, COALESCE(SUM(prints), 0) AS prints
FROM ranked
WHERE rn > 3
ORDER BY CASE WHEN name = 'Others' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, prints DESC, name;

WITH legal_pivot AS (
    SELECT
        l.uuid,
        l.format,
        MAX(l.id) FILTER (WHERE l.status = 'Legal')  AS legal_id,
        MAX(l.id) FILTER (WHERE l.status = 'Banned') AS banned_id
    FROM legalities AS l
    WHERE l.format IN ('commander', 'duel', 'legacy')
      AND l.status IN ('Legal', 'Banned')
    GROUP BY l.uuid, l.format
)
SELECT
    c.name AS card_name,
    p.format,
    p.legal_id,
    p.banned_id,
    p.legal_id - p.banned_id AS id_diff
FROM legal_pivot AS p
JOIN cards AS c
  ON c.uuid = p.uuid
ORDER BY c.name ASC, p.format ASC;

WITH base AS (
  SELECT name, title
  FROM customer_orders
  WHERE id = 1
  LIMIT 1
),
latest_update AS (
  SELECT name, title
  FROM updated_orders
  WHERE id = 1
  ORDER BY version_number DESC NULLS LAST, modified_at DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 1
)
INSERT INTO customer_orders (name, title)
SELECT
  COALESCE(uo.name, co.name) AS name,
  COALESCE(uo.title, co.title) AS title
FROM base co
FULL OUTER JOIN latest_update uo ON TRUE;

SELECT
  m.member_id AS link_to_member,
  TO_CHAR(MAX(e.event_date::timestamp), 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS latest_event_date
FROM member AS m
LEFT JOIN attendance AS a
  ON a.link_to_member = m.member_id
LEFT JOIN event AS e
  ON e.event_id = a.link_to_event
GROUP BY m.member_id
ORDER BY latest_event_date DESC NULLS LAST;

SELECT
  json_build_object(
    'customerid', c.customerid,
    'transactions', json_agg(t.transactionid ORDER BY t.transactionid)
  ) AS customer_json
FROM customers c
JOIN transactions_1k t
  ON t.customerid = c.customerid
WHERE c.segment = 'SME'
GROUP BY c.customerid;

WITH player_ranks AS (
  SELECT
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY overall_rating DESC NULLS LAST, player_api_id) AS rn,
    overall_rating AS player_overall_rating
  FROM player_attributes
  WHERE overall_rating IS NOT NULL
),
team_ranks AS (
  SELECT
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY buildupplayspeed DESC NULLS LAST, team_api_id) AS rn,
    buildupplayspeed AS team_buildupplayspeed
  FROM team_attributes
  WHERE buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  p.rn,
  p.player_overall_rating,
  t.team_buildupplayspeed
FROM player_ranks p
INNER JOIN team_ranks t
  ON p.rn = t.rn
ORDER BY p.rn;

SELECT
  e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS email_full_name,
  e.email,
  p.position
FROM member_email AS e
INNER JOIN member_position AS p
  ON (e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name) = p.full_name

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    e.id,
    e."Examination Date" AS as_of_date,
    e.diagnosis AS current_diagnosis,
    LAG(e.diagnosis) OVER (
      PARTITION BY e.id
      ORDER BY e."Examination Date"
    ) AS prev_diagnosis,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY e.id
      ORDER BY e."Examination Date"
    ) AS rn
  FROM examination e
),
change_points AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ordered
  WHERE rn = 1
     OR current_diagnosis IS DISTINCT FROM prev_diagnosis
),
target_changes AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM change_points
  WHERE current_diagnosis IN ('RA susp.', 'PSS', 'SLE')
)
SELECT
  id,
  MIN(as_of_date) AS first_target_date
FROM target_changes
GROUP BY id
ORDER BY id;

-- Create a temporary table and populate it with the first two rows as JSONB
CREATE TEMP TABLE destinationTable AS
SELECT jsonb_build_object('id', l.id, 'date', l."date") AS testRow

ORDER BY l.id, l."date"
LIMIT 2;

SELECT
  r.raceid,
  r.name AS racename,
  c.name AS circuitname,

  co.name AS constructorname,
  res.position AS finishposition,
  res.points
FROM results AS res
JOIN races AS r
  ON res.raceid = r.raceid
JOIN circuits AS c

JOIN drivers AS d

WHERE res.position IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY r.raceid, res.position;

WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    m."date" AS date_text,
    m.match_api_id AS code,  -- replace with your identifier if needed
    to_timestamp(m."date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::date AS match_d
  FROM "match" m
),
w AS (
  SELECT
    date_text,
    code,
    match_d,
    (CURRENT_DATE - match_d) AS duration,
    COUNT(*) OVER (
      PARTITION BY match_d, code, (CURRENT_DATE - match_d)
    ) AS cnt,
    MIN(CURRENT_DATE - match_d) OVER (
      PARTITION BY match_d, code
    ) AS min_dur
  FROM base
)
SELECT
  w.date_text AS "date",
  w.code,
  w.duration,
  CASE
    WHEN w.min_dur = 0 OR w.cnt > 1 THEN 'Y'
    ELSE 'N'
  END AS flg
FROM w
WHERE w.match_d = DATE '2008-11-22';

SELECT
  molecule_id,
  MIN(bond_type) AS smallest_bond_type
FROM bond
GROUP BY molecule_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT bond_type) > 1
ORDER BY molecule_id;

SELECT
