FROM superhero AS s
LEFT JOIN gender AS g
  ON g.id = s.gender_id
ORDER BY s.id;

BEGIN;

-- 1) Refresh backup from the current source
TRUNCATE TABLE treatment_and_followup_backup;

INSERT INTO treatment_and_followup_backup (
  treatment_followup_id,
  patient_id,
  followup_no,
  primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
  primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
  primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
  primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
  has_follow_ups_information,
  has_drugs_information,
  has_radiations_information,
  project,
  day_of_form_completion,
  month_of_form_completion,
  year_of_form_completion
)
SELECT
  treatment_followup_id,
  patient_id,
  followup_no,
  primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
  primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
  primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
  primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
  has_follow_ups_information,
  has_drugs_information,
  has_radiations_information,
  project,
  day_of_form_completion,
  month_of_form_completion,
  year_of_form_completion
FROM treatment_and_followup;

-- 2) Upsert source into target (latest)
MERGE INTO treatment_and_followup_latest AS t
USING treatment_and_followup AS s
ON (t.treatment_followup_id = s.treatment_followup_id)
WHEN MATCHED THEN
  UPDATE SET
    patient_id = s.patient_id,
    followup_no = s.followup_no,
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score = s.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group = s.primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy = s.primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx = s.primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    has_follow_ups_information = s.has_follow_ups_information,
    has_drugs_information = s.has_drugs_information,
    has_radiations_information = s.has_radiations_information,
    project = s.project,
    day_of_form_completion = s.day_of_form_completion,
    month_of_form_completion = s.month_of_form_completion,
    year_of_form_completion = s.year_of_form_completion
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN
  INSERT (
    treatment_followup_id,
    patient_id,
    followup_no,
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    has_follow_ups_information,
    has_drugs_information,
    has_radiations_information,
    project,
    day_of_form_completion,
    month_of_form_completion,
    year_of_form_completion
  )
  VALUES (
    s.treatment_followup_id,
    s.patient_id,
    s.followup_no,
    s.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score,
    s.primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group,
    s.primary_pathology_radiation_therapy,
    s.primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx,
    s.has_follow_ups_information,
    s.has_drugs_information,
    s.has_radiations_information,
    s.project,
    s.day_of_form_completion,
    s.month_of_form_completion,
    s.year_of_form_completion
  );

-- 3) Remove rows in target that no longer exist in source
DELETE FROM treatment_and_followup_latest t
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM treatment_and_followup s
  WHERE s.treatment_followup_id = t.treatment_followup_id
);

COMMIT;

SELECT nextval('budget_seq') AS next_budget_id
FROM generate_series(1, 10);

SELECT e.event_id,
       e.event_name,
       (
         SELECT STRING_AGG(DISTINCT a.link_to_member, ';')
         FROM attendance a
         WHERE a.link_to_event = e.event_id
       ) AS attendances,
       (
         SELECT STRING_AGG(DISTINCT b.category, ';')
         FROM budget b
         WHERE b.link_to_event = e.event_id
           AND b.category IS NOT NULL
       ) AS budget_category
FROM event e
WHERE e.event_id = 'reczhS8wix6Kzbp9P';

SELECT
  m.id,
  m."date",
  m.home_team_goal,
  LAG(m.home_team_goal) OVER (
    PARTITION BY m.home_team_api_id
    ORDER BY m."date"::timestamp, m.id
  ) AS prev_home_goal
FROM "match" AS m
WHERE m."date"::date <= DATE '2024-02-26'
ORDER BY m."date"::timestamp, m.id;

SELECT DISTINCT ON (pr.id)
       pr.id,
       pr.date,
       pr.overall_rating
FROM player_rate AS pr
ORDER BY pr.id,
         pr.date DESC NULLS LAST,
         pr.last_modified_timestamp DESC NULLS LAST,
         pr.created_timestamp DESC NULLS LAST,
         pr.record_uuid;

SELECT
  r.raceid,
  r.name,
  a.parts[1] AS name1,
  a.parts[2] AS name2,
  a.parts[3] AS name3,
  a.parts[4] AS name4
FROM races AS r
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT regexp_split_to_array(btrim(r.name), '\s+') AS parts
) AS a;

SELECT
  pa.id,
  p.player_name AS name,
  pa.player_api_id,
  pa.date,              -- or pa.date::timestamp if you prefer a timestamp
  pa.overall_rating,
  pa.potential,
  pa.preferred_foot
FROM player_attributes AS pa
JOIN player AS p
  ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
ORDER BY pa.id
LIMIT 10;

-- Use pgcrypto's SHA1 and truncate to 8 bytes (returns a bytea(8)); also show a 16-char hex form
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;

SELECT
  p.product_id,
  substring(digest(convert_to(p.product_id, 'UTF8'), 'sha1') for 8) AS hash8_bytes,
  encode(substring(digest(convert_to(p.product_id, 'UTF8'), 'sha1') for 8), 'hex') AS hash8_hex,
  length(substring(digest(convert_to(p.product_id, 'UTF8'), 'sha1') for 8)) AS hash_length_bytes
FROM products p;

WITH pages AS (
  SELECT 1 AS page_id, 1 AS power_id UNION ALL
  SELECT 1, 7 UNION ALL
  SELECT 2, 1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 3, 1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 3, 7 UNION ALL
  SELECT 3, 89 UNION ALL
  SELECT 4, 20 UNION ALL
  SELECT 5, 55 UNION ALL
  SELECT 5, 1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 6, 1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 6, 13
),
items AS (
  SELECT 10 AS hero_id, 1 AS power_id UNION ALL
  SELECT 10, 7 UNION ALL
  SELECT 10, 20 UNION ALL
  SELECT 12, 1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 12, 55
),
req AS (
  SELECT page_id, COUNT(DISTINCT power_id) AS req_cnt
  FROM pages
  GROUP BY page_id
),
matches AS (
  SELECT p.page_id, i.hero_id, COUNT(DISTINCT p.power_id) AS matched_cnt
  FROM pages p
  JOIN items i ON i.power_id = p.power_id
  GROUP BY p.page_id, i.hero_id
)
SELECT m.page_id, m.hero_id
FROM matches m
JOIN req r ON r.page_id = m.page_id
WHERE m.matched_cnt = r.req_cnt
ORDER BY m.page_id, m.hero_id;

WITH status_counts AS (
  SELECT r.driverid, r.statusid, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM results r
  GROUP BY r.driverid, r.statusid
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT sc.*,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY sc.driverid ORDER BY sc.cnt DESC, sc.statusid) AS rn
  FROM status_counts sc
)
SELECT d.driverid,
       d.forename,
       d.surname,
       s.status AS most_common_status,
       r.cnt AS occurrences
FROM ranked r
JOIN drivers d ON d.driverid = r.driverid
JOIN status s  ON s.statusid = r.statusid
WHERE r.rn = 1
ORDER BY d.driverid;

WITH home_matches AS (
  SELECT
    m.id,
    m.home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    m."date"::timestamp AS match_ts,
    m.home_team_goal,
    m.away_team_goal,
    (m.home_team_goal > m.away_team_goal) AS is_win
  FROM "match" m
  WHERE m.home_team_api_id IS NOT NULL
    AND m."date" IS NOT NULL
    AND m.home_team_goal IS NOT NULL
    AND m.away_team_goal IS NOT NULL
),
sequenced AS (
  SELECT
    hm.*,
    SUM(CASE WHEN is_win THEN 0 ELSE 1 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY match_ts, id) AS streak_id
  FROM home_matches hm
)
SELECT
  team_api_id,
  MIN(match_ts)::date AS start_date,
  MAX(match_ts)::date AS end_date,
  COUNT(*) AS match_count
FROM sequenced
WHERE is_win
GROUP BY team_api_id, streak_id
ORDER BY team_api_id, start_date;

WITH latest_lab AS (
  SELECT id, plt, wbc
  FROM (
    SELECT
      l.id,
      l.plt,
      l.wbc,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY l.id ORDER BY l."date" DESC NULLS LAST) AS rn
    FROM laboratory l
    WHERE l."date" <= CURRENT_DATE
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT
  p.id AS patientid,
  p.sex,
  p.birthday,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.id IS NOT NULL) AS examinationcount,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.thrombosis = 1) AS thrombosiscount,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.thrombosis = 0) AS nothrombosiscount,
  CASE WHEN ll.plt < 150 OR ll.wbc > 11 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS highriskflag
FROM patient p
LEFT JOIN examination e
  ON e.id = p.id
LEFT JOIN latest_lab ll
  ON ll.id = p.id
GROUP BY p.id, p.sex, p.birthday, ll.plt, ll.wbc
HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.id IS NOT NULL) > 0
ORDER BY thrombosiscount DESC, patientid;

WITH ordered_results AS (
  SELECT
    res.driverid,
    COALESCE(res.points, 0) AS points,
    r.raceid,
    r.year,
    r.round,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY res.driverid
      ORDER BY r.year, r.round, r.raceid
    ) AS career_race_number
  FROM results res
  JOIN races r ON r.raceid = res.raceid
),
first_five AS (
  SELECT *
  FROM ordered_results
  WHERE career_race_number <= 5
)
SELECT
  d.driverid,
  d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS drivername,
  MAX(CASE WHEN f.career_race_number = 1 THEN f.points END) AS race1_points,
  MAX(CASE WHEN f.career_race_number = 2 THEN f.points END) AS race2_points,
  MAX(CASE WHEN f.career_race_number = 3 THEN f.points END) AS race3_points,
  MAX(CASE WHEN f.career_race_number = 4 THEN f.points END) AS race4_points,
  MAX(CASE WHEN f.career_race_number = 5 THEN f.points END) AS race5_points
FROM first_five f
JOIN drivers d ON d.driverid = f.driverid
GROUP BY d.driverid, d.forename, d.surname
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename;

WITH years AS (
  SELECT y AS yr
  FROM generate_series(2008, 2016) AS gs(y)
),
yearly_counts AS (
  SELECT
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM bl."transact_dt_tm")::int AS yr,
    bl."bond_type",
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM "bond_list" bl
  WHERE bl."transact_dt_tm" IS NOT NULL
    AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM bl."transact_dt_tm")::int BETWEEN 2008 AND 2016
  GROUP BY 1, 2
)
SELECT
  y.yr AS "year",
  br."bond_type",
  COALESCE(c.cnt, 0) AS bond_count
FROM years y
CROSS JOIN "bond_ref" br
LEFT JOIN yearly_counts c
  ON c.yr = y.yr
 AND c."bond_type" = br."bond_type"
ORDER BY
  y.yr,
  br."bond_seq";

SELECT
  TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', "date"), 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
  COUNT(DISTINCT "date") AS distinct_days
FROM rulings
WHERE "date" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', "date")
ORDER BY DATE_TRUNC('month', "date");

SELECT
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ts, transactionid) AS row_num,
  transactionid,
  ts,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (LEAD(ts) OVER (ORDER BY ts, transactionid) - ts))::bigint AS seconds_to_next
FROM (
  SELECT
    t.transactionid,
    (t.date + t."time"::time) AS ts
  FROM transactions_1k t
  WHERE t.date IS NOT NULL AND t."time" IS NOT NULL
) AS s
ORDER BY ts, transactionid
LIMIT 100

WITH RECURSIVE t AS (
  SELECT
    e.id,
    e."Examination Date",
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY e."Examination Date") AS seqnum
  FROM examination AS e
),
cte AS (
  -- Seed with the first exam per patient
  SELECT
    t.id,
    t."Examination Date" AS exam_date,
    t.seqnum,
    'N'::text AS isrehire,
    t."Examination Date" AS anchordate
  FROM t
  WHERE t.seqnum = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Step to the next exam for the same patient
  SELECT
    t.id,
    t."Examination Date" AS exam_date,
    t.seqnum,
    CASE
      WHEN t."Examination Date" > cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year' THEN 'Y'
      ELSE 'N'
    END AS isrehire,
    CASE
      WHEN t."Examination Date" > cte.anchordate + INTERVAL '1 year' THEN t."Examination Date"
      ELSE cte.anchordate
    END AS anchordate
  FROM cte
  JOIN t
    ON t.id = cte.id
   AND t.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1
)
SELECT
  id,
  exam_date AS "Examination Date",
  seqnum,
  isrehire
FROM cte
ORDER BY id, seqnum;

WITH RECURSIVE lineage AS (
  -- Start at every set that has a direct parent
  SELECT
    s.id AS start_id,
    s.code AS start_code,
    s.parentcode AS next_code,
    (s.code || '-' || s.parentcode) AS path,
    ARRAY[s.code] AS visited
  FROM sets s
  WHERE s.parentcode IS NOT NULL

  UNION ALL

  -- Walk up the chain, appending each ancestor code
  SELECT
    l.start_id,
    l.start_code,
    p.parentcode AS next_code,
    l.path || CASE WHEN p.parentcode IS NOT NULL THEN '-' || p.parentcode ELSE '' END AS path,
    CASE WHEN p.code IS NOT NULL THEN l.visited || p.code ELSE l.visited END AS visited
  FROM lineage l
  LEFT JOIN sets p
    ON p.code = l.next_code
  -- prevent cycles; allow termination when parent row is missing
  WHERE p.code IS NULL OR NOT (p.code = ANY(l.visited))
)
SELECT
  start_id AS id,
  start_code AS code,
  path AS ancestralCode
FROM lineage
WHERE next_code IS NULL
ORDER BY code;

WITH hero_activity AS (
    -- Sample data
    SELECT id, activity_date::date, activity_value, hero_id
    FROM (
        VALUES
            (1, '2023-01-01', 10, 1),
            (2, '2023-01-02', 20, 1),
            (3, '2023-02-01', 15, 1),
            (4, '2023-02-02', 25, 1),
            (5, '2023-01-03', 30, 2),
            (6, '2023-02-03', 40, 2)
    ) AS t (id, activity_date, activity_value, hero_id)
),
-- Aggregate totals per calendar month
monthly_raw AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('month', activity_date)::date AS month_start,
           SUM(activity_value) AS month_total
    FROM hero_activity
    GROUP BY 1
),
-- Create a complete month series to handle months with no data (treated as 0 total)
bounds AS (
    SELECT MIN(month_start) AS min_month, MAX(month_start) AS max_month
    FROM monthly_raw
),
month_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
               (SELECT min_month FROM bounds),
               (SELECT max_month FROM bounds),
               interval '1 month'
           )::date AS month_start
),
monthly_filled AS (
    SELECT s.month_start,
           COALESCE(m.month_total, 0) AS month_total
    FROM month_series s
    LEFT JOIN monthly_raw m USING (month_start)
),
-- Compute previous calendar month's total
months_with_prev AS (
    SELECT month_start,
           month_total,
           LAG(month_total) OVER (ORDER BY month_start) AS prev_month_total
    FROM monthly_filled
)
SELECT
    h.id,
    h.activity_date,
    h.activity_value,
    -- 1) Running total across all records
    SUM(h.activity_value) OVER (
        ORDER BY h.activity_date, h.id
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS run_tot,
    -- 2) Running total within each calendar month
    SUM(h.activity_value) OVER (
        PARTITION BY date_trunc('month', h.activity_date)
        ORDER BY h.activity_date, h.id
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS m_run_tot,
    -- 3) Total of the previous calendar month
    mwp.prev_month_total AS prev_month_total
FROM hero_activity h
JOIN months_with_prev mwp
  ON date_trunc('month', h.activity_date)::date = mwp.month_start
ORDER BY h.activity_date, h.id;

WITH ranked_dates AS (
  SELECT
    m.country_id,
    m.date::timestamp AS match_ts,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (
      PARTITION BY m.country_id
      ORDER BY m.date::timestamp DESC
    ) AS rnk
  FROM "match" m
  WHERE m.date IS NOT NULL
),
top2 AS (
  SELECT country_id, match_ts, rnk
  FROM ranked_dates
  WHERE rnk <= 2
)
SELECT
  c.id AS country_id,
  c.name AS country_name,
  MAX(CASE WHEN t.rnk = 1 THEN t.match_ts END) AS last_match_date,
  MAX(CASE WHEN t.rnk = 2 THEN t.match_ts END) AS prev_match_date
FROM country c
JOIN top2 t ON t.country_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
ORDER BY last_match_date DESC

SELECT
  a1.atom_id AS atom_id1,
  a2.atom_id AS atom_id2,
  b.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM atom AS a1
JOIN atom AS a2
  ON a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id
 AND a1.atom_id < a2.atom_id            -- unique unordered pairs, no self-pairs
LEFT JOIN (
  -- canonicalize connections so either direction (a,b) or (b,a) matches the same pair
  SELECT DISTINCT
    LEAST(atom_id, atom_id2)  AS atom_low,
    GREATEST(atom_id, atom_id2) AS atom_high,
    bond_id
  FROM connected
) c
  ON c.atom_low = a1.atom_id
 AND c.atom_high = a2.atom_id
LEFT JOIN bond AS b
  ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
ORDER BY a1.atom_id, a2.atom_id;

WITH snapshots AS (
  SELECT
    cdscode,
    lastupdate,
    opendate,
    school,
    fundingtype,
    NULLIF(BTRIM(school), '')      AS school_norm,
    NULLIF(BTRIM(fundingtype), '') AS funding_norm
  FROM schools
),
sequenced AS (
  SELECT
    s.*,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY cdscode
      ORDER BY lastupdate, opendate NULLS FIRST, school_norm, funding_norm, school, fundingtype
    ) AS rn,
    LAG(s.school_norm) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cdscode
      ORDER BY lastupdate, opendate NULLS FIRST, school_norm, funding_norm, school, fundingtype
    ) AS prev_school_norm,
    LAG(s.funding_norm) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cdscode
      ORDER BY lastupdate, opendate NULLS FIRST, school_norm, funding_norm, school, fundingtype
    ) AS prev_funding_norm,
    LAG(s.school) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cdscode
      ORDER BY lastupdate, opendate NULLS FIRST, school_norm, funding_norm, school, fundingtype
    ) AS prev_school_raw,
    LAG(s.fundingtype) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cdscode
      ORDER BY lastupdate, opendate NULLS FIRST, school_norm, funding_norm, school, fundingtype
    ) AS prev_funding_raw
  FROM snapshots s
),
changes AS (
  SELECT
    cdscode,
    lastupdate,
    prev_school_raw,
    school           AS curr_school_raw,
    prev_funding_raw,
    fundingtype      AS curr_funding_raw,
    prev_school_norm,
    school_norm,
    prev_funding_norm,
    funding_norm
  FROM sequenced
  WHERE rn > 1
    AND (
      school_norm  IS DISTINCT FROM prev_school_norm
      OR
      funding_norm IS DISTINCT FROM prev_funding_norm
    )
)
SELECT
  cdscode,
  'School' AS ColumnName,
  COALESCE(prev_school_raw, '') AS OldValue,
  COALESCE(curr_school_raw, '') AS NewValue
FROM changes
WHERE school_norm IS DISTINCT FROM prev_school_norm

UNION ALL

SELECT
  cdscode,
  'FundingType' AS ColumnName,
  COALESCE(prev_funding_raw, '') AS OldValue,
  COALESCE(curr_funding_raw, '') AS NewValue
FROM changes
WHERE funding_norm IS DISTINCT FROM prev_funding_norm

ORDER BY cdscode, lastupdate, ColumnName;

WITH RECURSIVE t_period_futur AS (
  SELECT 1 AS tpf_period_id,
         DATE '2021-10-25' AS tpf_start_period
  UNION ALL
  SELECT tpf_period_id + 1,
         (tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '1 week')::date
  FROM t_period_futur
  WHERE tpf_period_id < 12
)
SELECT
  tpf_period_id,
  tpf_start_period,
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM tpf_start_period) AS tpf_year,
  to_char(tpf_start_period, 'YYYY') || '.' || to_char(tpf_start_period, 'IW') AS tpf_period_number,
  (tpf_start_period + INTERVAL '6 days')::date AS tpf_end_period
FROM t_period_futur
ORDER BY tpf_period_id;

WITH exams AS (
  SELECT
    e.id,
    e."Examination Date" AS exam_date,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', e."Examination Date")::date AS month_start,
    e.thrombosis,
    LAG(e.thrombosis) OVER (
      PARTITION BY e.id
      ORDER BY e."Examination Date"
    ) AS prev_thrombosis,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY e.id, DATE_TRUNC('month', e."Examination Date")
      ORDER BY e."Examination Date" DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM examination e
)
SELECT
  id,
  month_start,
  CASE
    WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NOT NULL AND thrombosis > prev_thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
    ELSE 'No'
  END AS increase_risk,
  CASE
    WHEN prev_thrombosis IS NOT NULL AND thrombosis < prev_thrombosis THEN 'Yes'
    ELSE 'No'
  END AS decrease_risk
FROM exams
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY id, month_start;

WITH m AS (
  SELECT
    to_timestamp("date", 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS') AS ts,
    "home_team_goal",
    "away_team_goal"
  FROM "match"
)
SELECT
  to_char(ts, 'IYYY')::int AS iso_year,
  to_char(ts, 'IW')::int AS iso_week,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE home_team_goal > away_team_goal) AS home_wins,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE home_team_goal < away_team_goal) AS away_wins,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE home_team_goal = away_team_goal) AS draws,
  COUNT(*) AS total_matches
FROM m
WHERE home_team_goal IS NOT NULL
  AND away_team_goal IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY 1, 2;

SELECT
  json_object_agg('country_id' || country_id::text, match_list) AS json
FROM (
  SELECT
    m.country_id,
    json_agg(
      json_build_object(
        'date', m.date,
        'home_team_goal', m.home_team_goal,
        'away_team_goal', m.away_team_goal
      )
      ORDER BY m.date::timestamp
    ) AS match_list
  FROM "match" m
  WHERE m.country_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY m.country_id

SELECT
  r.raceid,
  r.year,
  r.name AS racename,
  MAX(CASE WHEN res.position = 1 THEN res.driverid END) AS position1,
  MAX(CASE WHEN res.position = 2 THEN res.driverid END) AS position2,
  MAX(CASE WHEN res.position = 3 THEN res.driverid END) AS position3
FROM races AS r
JOIN results AS res ON res.raceid = r.raceid
WHERE res.position IN (1, 2, 3)
GROUP BY r.raceid, r.year, r.name
ORDER BY r.year DESC, r.round, r.raceid;

INSERT INTO "groups" (room_guid, user_guid)
WITH duplicated_pairs AS (
  SELECT login, name
  FROM "login"
  GROUP BY login, name
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
existing_map AS (
  SELECT l.login, l.name, g.user_guid
  FROM duplicated_pairs d
  JOIN "login" l
    ON l.login IS NOT DISTINCT FROM d.login
   AND l.name  IS NOT DISTINCT FROM d.name
  JOIN "groups" g ON g.room_guid = l.guid
),
unambiguous_map AS (
  SELECT login, name, MIN(user_guid) AS user_guid
  FROM existing_map
  GROUP BY login, name
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT user_guid) = 1
),
missing AS (
  SELECT l.guid AS missing_guid, l.login, l.name
  FROM duplicated_pairs d
  JOIN "login" l
    ON l.login IS NOT DISTINCT FROM d.login
   AND l.name  IS NOT DISTINCT FROM d.name
  LEFT JOIN "groups" g ON g.room_guid = l.guid
  WHERE g.room_guid IS NULL
)
SELECT m.missing_guid, um.user_guid
FROM missing m
JOIN unambiguous_map um
  ON um.login IS NOT DISTINCT FROM m.login
 AND um.name  IS NOT DISTINCT FROM m.name;

WITH runs AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    number,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN number IS DISTINCT FROM LAG(number) OVER (ORDER BY id)
          THEN 1
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) OVER (ORDER BY id) AS grp
  FROM cards
  WHERE number IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  number,
  MIN(id) AS start_id,
  MAX(id) AS end_id,
  COUNT(*) AS run_length
FROM runs
GROUP BY grp, number
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY start_id;

WITH RECURSIVE
req AS (
  -- Aggregate required qty of material 2 per experiment (in case of duplicates)
  SELECT e.exp_id, 2::int AS mid, SUM(e.qty)::int AS qty
  FROM experiment e
  WHERE e.mid = 2
  GROUP BY e.exp_id
),
ordered AS (
  -- Order experiments by ID to process sequentially
  SELECT exp_id, mid, qty, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) AS rn
  FROM req
),
inv AS (
  -- Starting stock for material 2 (0 if missing)
  SELECT COALESCE((SELECT i.qty FROM inventory i WHERE i.mid = 2), 0)::int AS start_qty
),
rec AS (
  -- Anchor: first experiment
  SELECT
    o.exp_id,
    o.mid,
    o.qty,
    CASE WHEN o.qty <= inv.start_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS executable,
    CASE WHEN o.qty <= inv.start_qty THEN inv.start_qty - o.qty ELSE inv.start_qty END AS remaining_qty,
    o.rn
  FROM ordered o
  CROSS JOIN inv
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Recursive step: next experiment consumes only if previous was executable
  SELECT
    o.exp_id,
    o.mid,
    o.qty,
    CASE WHEN o.qty <= r.remaining_qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS executable,
    CASE WHEN o.qty <= r.remaining_qty THEN r.remaining_qty - o.qty ELSE r.remaining_qty END AS remaining_qty,
    o.rn
  FROM ordered o
  JOIN rec r ON o.rn = r.rn + 1
)
SELECT exp_id, mid, executable
FROM rec
ORDER BY rn;

WITH
-- Molecules that have the specific bond (avoids scalar subquery errors)
molecules_with_target_bond AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT b.molecule_id
  FROM bond b
  WHERE b.bond_id = 'TR011_2_24'
),

-- Molecules that have at least one connected atom pair with the same element
molecules_with_equal_atom_connections AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT a1.molecule_id
  FROM connected c
  JOIN atom a1 ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
  JOIN atom a2 ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
  WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id
    AND a1.element = a2.element
),

-- Set A: molecules filtered by bond + equal-element connection, with element counts
set_a AS (
  SELECT at.molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT at.element) AS element_count
  FROM atom at
  JOIN molecules_with_target_bond tb ON tb.molecule_id = at.molecule_id
  JOIN molecules_with_equal_atom_connections me ON me.molecule_id = at.molecule_id
  GROUP BY at.molecule_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT at.element) > 1
),

-- Set B: molecules with any label, with element counts
set_b AS (
  SELECT m.molecule_id, COUNT(DISTINCT a.element) AS element_count
  FROM molecule m
  JOIN atom a ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
  WHERE m.label IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY m.molecule_id
),

-- Rank both sets independently (rank by element_count, descending)
ranked_a AS (
  SELECT molecule_id,
         element_count,
         DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
  FROM set_a
),
ranked_b AS (
  SELECT molecule_id,
         element_count,
         DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY element_count DESC) AS density_rank
  FROM set_b
),

-- Pair by molecule_id to compare ranks
paired AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(ra.molecule_id, rb.molecule_id) AS molecule_id,
    ra.density_rank AS rank_a,
    rb.density_rank AS rank_b
  FROM ranked_a ra
  FULL JOIN ranked_b rb
    ON ra.molecule_id = rb.molecule_id
)

-- Final counts:
-- 1) identical in both (same molecule_id AND same rank)
-- 2) only in A (molecule in A with no matching (id, rank) in B)
-- 3) only in B (molecule in B with no matching (id, rank) in A)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE rank_a IS NOT NULL AND rank_b IS NOT NULL AND rank_a = rank_b) AS num_identical_in_a_and_b,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE rank_a IS NOT NULL AND (rank_b IS NULL OR rank_a <> rank_b)) AS num_only_in_a,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE rank_b IS NOT NULL AND (rank_a IS NULL OR rank_a <> rank_b)) AS num_only_in_b
FROM paired;

WITH keywordmatches AS (
  SELECT
    p.id,
    CASE WHEN POSITION('aaron' IN LOWER(p.player_name)) > 0 THEN 'Aaron' END AS colakeyword,
    CASE WHEN POSITION('cresswell' IN LOWER(p.player_name)) > 0 THEN 'Cresswell' END AS colbkeyword,
    CASE WHEN POSITION('doran' IN LOWER(p.player_name)) > 0 THEN 'Doran' END AS colckeyword
  FROM player AS p
),
keywordscores AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    SUM(CASE WHEN colakeyword IS NOT NULL THEN 10 ELSE 0 END) AS colascore,
    SUM(CASE WHEN colbkeyword IS NOT NULL THEN 5 ELSE 0 END) AS colbscore,
    SUM(CASE WHEN colckeyword IS NOT NULL THEN 0.1 ELSE 0 END) AS colcscore
  FROM keywordmatches
  GROUP BY id
)
SELECT
  p.id,
  p.player_name AS description,
  (ks.colascore + ks.colbscore + ks.colcscore) AS score
FROM player AS p
JOIN keywordscores AS ks ON p.id = ks.id
ORDER BY score DESC, p.player_name;

SELECT p.id, s.value AS diagnosis_code
FROM patient AS p
CROSS JOIN LATERAL regexp_split_to_table(p.diagnosis, '\s*,\s*') AS s(value)
WHERE lower(s.value) = lower($1);

WITH schema_info AS (
  SELECT
    c.column_name,
    c.data_type,
    c.ordinal_position
  FROM information_schema.columns c
  WHERE c.table_schema = current_schema()
    AND c.table_name  = 'schools'
),
sat_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.cds,
    s.rtype
  FROM satscores s
  WHERE s.numtsttakr > 0
)
SELECT xmlserialize(content
  xmlelement(name "Results",
    xmlelement(name "Columns",
      (
        SELECT xmlagg(
                 xmlelement(name "Column",
                   xmlattributes(si.column_name as "Name"),
                   xmlelement(name "DataType", si.data_type)
                 )
                 ORDER BY si.ordinal_position
               )
        FROM schema_info si
      )
    ),
    xmlelement(name "Rows",
      (
        SELECT xmlagg(
                 xmlelement(name "Row",
                   xmlelement(name "cds",  sd.cds),
                   xmlelement(name "rtype", sd.rtype)
                 )
                 ORDER BY sd.cds
               )
        FROM sat_data sd
      )
    )
  ) AS text
) AS xml_text;

-- Drop child first to avoid dependency errors, then parent
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "BusinessCategories";
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "EventCategories";

-- Parent (general categories)
CREATE TABLE "EventCategories" (
  "ID" INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
  "IsBusinessCategory" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  -- Needed so the FK in the child can reference (ID, IsBusinessCategory)
  CONSTRAINT uq_eventcategories_id_isbiz UNIQUE ("ID", "IsBusinessCategory")
);

-- Child (business-only categories)
CREATE TABLE "BusinessCategories" (
  "ID" INT GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY,
  "BusinessCategoryID" INT NOT NULL,
  -- Always true in this table; enforced by CHECK
  "IsBusinessCategory" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  CONSTRAINT chk_business_isbiz_true CHECK ("IsBusinessCategory" = true),
  CONSTRAINT fk_business_to_event_isbiz
    FOREIGN KEY ("BusinessCategoryID", "IsBusinessCategory")
    REFERENCES "EventCategories" ("ID", "IsBusinessCategory")
    ON UPDATE RESTRICT
    ON DELETE RESTRICT
);

WITH bounds AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    rate::numeric AS rate,
    lower(time_span) AS ts_start,
    lower(time_span)::date AS start_date,
    CASE
      WHEN upper(time_span) IS NULL THEN now()::date
      ELSE (upper(time_span) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date  -- upper() is exclusive; make inclusive
    END AS end_date_incl
  FROM budgets
  WHERE lower(time_span) IS NOT NULL
),
month_segments AS (
  SELECT
    b.id,
    b.rate,
    gs.anchor::date AS seg_start_date,
    LEAST(((gs.anchor + INTERVAL '1 month')::date), b.end_date_incl + 1) AS seg_end_excl_date
  FROM bounds b
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
    b.start_date::timestamp,
    b.end_date_incl::timestamp,
    INTERVAL '1 month'
  ) AS gs(anchor)
),
daily AS (
  SELECT
    s.id,
    d::date AS dt,
    ROUND(s.rate / GREATEST((s.seg_end_excl_date - s.seg_start_date), 1), 2) AS daily_rate
  FROM month_segments s
  CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
    s.seg_start_date::timestamp,
    (s.seg_end_excl_date - 1)::timestamp,
    INTERVAL '1 day'
  ) AS g(d)
)
SELECT
  dt::date AS date,
  daily_rate
FROM daily
ORDER BY dt;

-- Include a small "grace window" before the target day to catch late-previous-day timestamps
WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    timestamp '2010-02-22 00:00:00' AS day_start,
    interval '1 hour' AS grace -- adjust if needed
)
SELECT ta.*
FROM team_attributes AS ta
CROSS JOIN params p
WHERE ta.date::timestamp >= p.day_start - p.grace
  AND ta.date::timestamp < p.day_start + interval '1 day';

WITH params AS (
  SELECT 10::int AS cap, 7::int AS max_items
),
my_table AS (
  SELECT 'A'::text AS cto, 1::int AS nif, 'A1'::text AS file_, 5::int AS filesize
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 1, 'A2', 3
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A3', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'A', 2, 'A4', 1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B1', 4
  UNION ALL SELECT 'B', 1, 'B2', 6
),
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    t.*,
    row_number() OVER (
      PARTITION BY cto
      ORDER BY substring(file_ FROM 2)::int, file_
    ) AS rn,
    lag(nif) OVER (
      PARTITION BY cto
      ORDER BY substring(file_ FROM 2)::int, file_
    ) AS prev_nif
  FROM my_table t
),
rows_with_block AS (
  SELECT
    o.*,
    /* consecutive block id: increments only when nif changes within cto */
    sum(CASE WHEN prev_nif IS DISTINCT FROM nif THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY rn ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS block_id
  FROM ordered o
),
blocks AS (
  /* One row per (cto, block) with its total size and natural order */
  SELECT
    cto,
    block_id,
    min(rn) AS block_first_rn,
    sum(filesize)::int AS block_size
  FROM rows_with_block
  GROUP BY cto, block_id
),
blocks_with_running AS (
  SELECT
    b.*,
    /* cumulative size up to and including this block */
    sum(block_size) OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY block_id
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cum_size
  FROM blocks b
),
mn_groups AS (
  /* First-level grouping (subgroups): add entire next block only if cap won't be exceeded.
     Using: group_id = floor((cum_with_current - 1) / cap) + 1 */
  SELECT
    bw.cto,
    bw.block_id,
    bw.block_size,
    bw.block_first_rn,
    ((bw.cum_size - 1) / p.cap) + 1 AS mn
  FROM blocks_with_running bw
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
final_groups AS (
  /* Second-level grouping: limit to max_items subgroups per final partition */
  SELECT
    g.*,
    ((g.mn - 1) / p.max_items) + 1 AS partition,
    ((g.mn - 1) % p.max_items) + 1 AS subpartition
  FROM mn_groups g
  CROSS JOIN params p
),
rows_tagged AS (
  SELECT
    r.cto,
    r.nif,
    r.file_,
    fg.partition,
    fg.subpartition
  FROM rows_with_block r
  JOIN final_groups fg
    ON fg.cto = r.cto
   AND fg.block_id = r.block_id
)
SELECT
  rt.cto,
  rt.nif,
  rt.file_,
  rt.partition,
  rt.subpartition,
  /* total size of all files in the final (cto, partition) */
  sum(t.filesize) OVER (PARTITION BY rt.cto, rt.partition)::int AS total_size
FROM rows_tagged rt
JOIN my_table t
  ON t.cto = rt.cto
 AND t.file_ = rt.file_
ORDER BY
  rt.cto,
  substring(rt.file_ FROM 2)::int,
  rt.file_;

WITH ranges AS (
    -- Replace these sample rows with your source table of ranges
    -- (valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE)
    SELECT DATE '2014-01-01' AS valid_from, DATE '2014-01-31' AS valid_until
    UNION ALL
    SELECT DATE '2014-02-01', DATE '2014-02-28'
),
events AS (
    -- +1 when a range starts
    SELECT valid_from::timestamp AS dt, 1 AS delta
    FROM ranges
    UNION ALL
    -- -1 the day after a range ends (to model inclusive end dates)
    SELECT (valid_until + INTERVAL '1 day')::timestamp AS dt, -1 AS delta
    FROM ranges
    WHERE valid_until IS NOT NULL
),
event_days AS (
    -- Aggregate all events that happen on the same day
    SELECT dt::date AS dt, SUM(delta) AS delta_sum
    FROM events
    GROUP BY dt::date
),
timeline AS (
    -- Build continuous periods and running active count
    SELECT
        dt AS valid_from,
        (LEAD(dt) OVER (ORDER BY dt) - INTERVAL '1 day')::date AS valid_until,
        SUM(delta_sum) OVER (ORDER BY dt ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS active_count
    FROM event_days
)
SELECT
    valid_from,
    valid_until,
    active_count AS count
FROM timeline
WHERE active_count > 0
  AND (valid_until IS NULL OR valid_from <= valid_until)
ORDER BY valid_from;

-- Fix the trigger function to be statement-level safe and use a transition table
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS insert_race_name_trigger ON races;
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS insert_race_name();

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION insert_race_name()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- Insert one row into race_names for each race inserted by the statement
  INSERT INTO race_names (race_name)
  SELECT DISTINCT name
  FROM new_rows
  WHERE name IS NOT NULL;

  -- For statement-level AFTER triggers the return value is ignored; return NULL
  RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;

-- Recreate the trigger as AFTER INSERT, statement-level, with a transition table
CREATE TRIGGER insert_race_name_trigger
AFTER INSERT ON races
REFERENCING NEW TABLE AS new_rows
FOR EACH STATEMENT
EXECUTE FUNCTION insert_race_name();

WITH ordered_atoms AS (
  SELECT
    a.atom_id,
    a.element,
    LEAD(a.atom_id) OVER (
      ORDER BY split_part(a.atom_id, '_', 2)::int
    ) AS next_atom_id
  FROM atom a
  WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR000'
)
SELECT
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY split_part(oa.atom_id, '_', 2)::int) AS seq_no,
  oa.atom_id,
  oa.element,
  na.atom_id AS next_atom_id,
  na.element AS next_element,
  c.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM ordered_atoms oa
LEFT JOIN atom na
  ON na.atom_id = oa.next_atom_id
LEFT JOIN connected c
  ON c.atom_id = oa.atom_id
 AND c.atom_id2 = oa.next_atom_id
LEFT JOIN bond b
  ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
ORDER BY seq_no;

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    t.transactionid,
    t.customerid,
    t.startdate,
    t.enddate,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY t.customerid
      ORDER BY t.startdate, t.enddate, t.transactionid
    ) AS rn
  FROM transactions_1k t
),
rec AS (
  -- First transaction per customer: always unflagged (0)
  SELECT
    o.customerid,
    o.transactionid,
    o.startdate,
    o.enddate,
    0::int AS flag,
    o.enddate AS ref_enddate,
    o.rn
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Subsequent transactions: compare startdate to last unflagged ref_enddate + 90 days
  SELECT
    o.customerid,
    o.transactionid,
    o.startdate,
    o.enddate,
    CASE
      WHEN o.startdate <= rec.ref_enddate + 90 THEN 1
      ELSE 0
    END AS flag,
    CASE
      -- If flagged (within 90 days), keep ref_enddate from last unflagged
      WHEN o.startdate <= rec.ref_enddate + 90 THEN rec.ref_enddate
      -- If unflagged (outside 90 days), move ref_enddate to this transaction's enddate
      ELSE o.enddate
    END AS ref_enddate,
    o.rn
  FROM rec
  JOIN ordered o
    ON o.customerid = rec.customerid
   AND o.rn = rec.rn + 1
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  startdate,
  enddate,
  flag
FROM rec
ORDER BY customerid, startdate, enddate, transactionid;

WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN originalreleasedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN to_date(originalreleasedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')
      WHEN originalreleasedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}$' THEN to_date(originalreleasedate || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
      WHEN originalreleasedate ~ '^\d{4}$' THEN to_date(originalreleasedate || '-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
      ELSE NULL
    END AS release_date
  FROM cards
  WHERE originalreleasedate IS NOT NULL
),
bounds AS (
  SELECT
    DATE '2007-01-01' AS start_date,
    GREATEST(DATE '2007-01-01', COALESCE(MAX(release_date), DATE '2007-01-01')) AS end_date
  FROM parsed
),
weeks AS (
  SELECT generate_series(b.start_date, b.end_date, INTERVAL '1 week')::date AS week_start
  FROM bounds b
)
SELECT
  w.week_start AS start_of_week,
  ((w.week_start - DATE '2007-01-01') / 7)::int + 1 AS week_number,
  COUNT(p.release_date) AS card_count
FROM weeks w
LEFT JOIN parsed p
  ON p.release_date >= w.week_start
 AND p.release_date < (w.week_start + INTERVAL '1 week')
GROUP BY w.week_start
ORDER BY w.week_start;

WITH per_exam AS (
  SELECT
    e."Examination Date" AS exam_date,
    -- Canonical (sorted, deduplicated) representation of the patients linked to this exam date
    array_agg(DISTINCT p_id ORDER BY p_id) AS patient_set
  FROM examination e
  JOIN (
    -- Patients with an examination on this date
    SELECT "Examination Date" AS dt, id AS p_id
    FROM examination
    WHERE id IS NOT NULL

    UNION

    -- Patients with a laboratory record on this date
    SELECT date AS dt, id AS p_id
    FROM laboratory
    WHERE id IS NOT NULL
  ) x
    ON x.dt = e."Examination Date"
  GROUP BY e."Examination Date"
)
-- Count distinct sets across all exam records (dates)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT patient_set) AS unique_patient_groups
FROM per_exam;

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    driverid,
    contracttype,
    startdate::date AS startdate,
    enddate::date   AS enddate
  FROM drivercontracts
),
running AS (
  SELECT
    driverid,
    contracttype,
    startdate,
    enddate,
    max(enddate) OVER (
      PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype
      ORDER BY startdate, enddate
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS max_end_so_far
  FROM ordered
),
flags AS (
  SELECT
    driverid,
    contracttype,
    startdate,
    enddate,
    CASE
      -- same driver + type: start a new group only if there's a gap after the running max end
      WHEN startdate <= lag(max_end_so_far) OVER (
             PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype
             ORDER BY startdate, enddate
           ) + 1
      THEN 0 ELSE 1
    END AS new_group
  FROM running
),
groups AS (
  SELECT
    driverid,
    contracttype,
    startdate,
    enddate,
    sum(new_group) OVER (
      PARTITION BY driverid, contracttype
      ORDER BY startdate, enddate
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS grp
  FROM flags
)
SELECT
  driverid,
  contracttype,
  min(startdate) AS period_start,
  max(enddate)   AS period_end
FROM groups
GROUP BY driverid, contracttype, grp
ORDER BY driverid, contracttype, period_start;

WITH events AS (
  -- Build +1/-1 events for starts/ends (treating end_date as inclusive)
  SELECT start_date AS d, 1 AS active_delta, 0 AS blackout_delta
  FROM account_activity
  UNION ALL
  SELECT end_date + 1 AS d, -1 AS active_delta, 0 AS blackout_delta
  FROM account_activity
  UNION ALL
  SELECT start_date AS d, 0 AS active_delta, 1 AS blackout_delta
  FROM blackout_periods
  UNION ALL
  SELECT end_date + 1 AS d, 0 AS active_delta, -1 AS blackout_delta
  FROM blackout_periods
),
events_agg AS (
  -- Collapse coincident events to a single row per date
  SELECT d,
         SUM(active_delta)   AS active_delta,
         SUM(blackout_delta) AS blackout_delta
  FROM events
  GROUP BY d
),
timeline AS (
  -- Compute running counts of "active" and "blackout" at each boundary date
  SELECT d,
         SUM(active_delta)   OVER (ORDER BY d) AS active_count,
         SUM(blackout_delta) OVER (ORDER BY d) AS blackout_count
  FROM events_agg
),
segments AS (
  -- Transform boundary points into segments between consecutive dates
  SELECT d AS seg_start,
         LEAD(d) OVER (ORDER BY d) AS seg_end,
         active_count,
         blackout_count
  FROM timeline
)
-- Keep only those segments where we are inside activity (active_count > 0)
-- and not in any blackout (blackout_count = 0). Convert back to inclusive end_date.
SELECT seg_start AS start_date,
       (seg_end - 1) AS end_date
FROM segments
WHERE seg_end IS NOT NULL
  AND active_count > 0
  AND blackout_count = 0
ORDER BY start_date;

      THEN ('00:' || r.fastestlaptime)::interval
      ELSE NULL
    END AS fl_interval
  FROM results r
  JOIN drivers d ON d.driverid = r.driverid
)
SELECT
  ranked.*,
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    ORDER BY
      fl_interval ASC NULLS LAST,     -- lower fastest lap time first

  ) AS ranking

-- One row per unique connection (pair of atoms), with both atoms and the bond
SELECT
  m.molecule_id,
  m.label,
  a1.atom_id  AS atom_id_1,
  a1.element  AS element_1,
  a2.atom_id  AS atom_id_2,
  a2.element  AS element_2,
  b.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM molecule   AS m
JOIN bond       AS b  ON b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
JOIN connected  AS c  ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id              -- correct FK join: connection -> bond
JOIN atom       AS a1 ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
                     AND a1.molecule_id = m.molecule_id      -- ensure atom belongs to the same molecule
JOIN atom       AS a2 ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
                     AND a2.molecule_id = m.molecule_id      -- ensure atom belongs to the same molecule
WHERE a1.atom_id < a2.atom_id                                 -- keep only one direction of each pair
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, b.bond_id, a1.atom_id, a2.atom_id;

WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM matches.ts) BETWEEN 2008 AND 2016
GROUP BY l.id, l.name, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM matches.ts)
ORDER BY year, league_name;

SELECT f.team,
       f.source AS first_source,
       l.destination AS final_destination
FROM (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (team) team, source
  FROM source_destination_flight
  WHERE departure_time IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY team, departure_time ASC, id ASC
) AS f
JOIN (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (team) team, destination
  FROM source_destination_flight
  WHERE arrival_time IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY team, arrival_time DESC NULLS LAST, id DESC
) AS l
  ON l.team = f.team
ORDER BY f.team;

SELECT c.id
FROM cards AS c
WHERE c.id <> 1
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM decks AS d
    WHERE d.card2_id = c.id
      AND d.status IS NULL
  )
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM decks AS d_excl
    WHERE d_excl.card1_id = 1
      AND d_excl.card2_id = c.id
  );

SELECT atom_id, molecule_id, element
FROM (
  SELECT
    a.atom_id,
    a.molecule_id,
    a.element,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY a.molecule_id
      -- Use numeric part of atom_id so TR000_10 comes after TR000_9
      ORDER BY split_part(a.atom_id, '_', 2)::int
    ) AS rn
  FROM atom a
) ranked
WHERE rn <= 2
ORDER BY molecule_id, rn;

SELECT
  m."molecule_id",
  COALESCE(a.atom_cnt, 0) * 1.0 / NULLIF(b.bond_cnt, 0) AS result
FROM "molecule" m
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT "molecule_id", COUNT(*) AS atom_cnt
  FROM "atom"
  GROUP BY "molecule_id"
) a ON a."molecule_id" = m."molecule_id"
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT "molecule_id", COUNT(*) AS bond_cnt
  FROM "bond"
  GROUP BY "molecule_id"
) b ON b."molecule_id" = m."molecule_id"

WITH per_year AS (
  SELECT
    e.type AS event_type,
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM e.event_date::timestamp)::int AS event_year,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM event e
  WHERE e.event_date IS NOT NULL
    AND e.type IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY e.type, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM e.event_date::timestamp)
),
bounds AS (
  SELECT
    event_type,
    MIN(event_year) AS first_year,
    MAX(event_year) AS last_year
  FROM per_year
  GROUP BY event_type
)
SELECT
  b.event_type,
  COALESCE(l.cnt, 0) - COALESCE(f.cnt, 0) AS year_difference
FROM bounds b
LEFT JOIN per_year f
  ON f.event_type = b.event_type AND f.event_year = b.first_year
LEFT JOIN per_year l
  ON l.event_type = b.event_type AND l.event_year = b.last_year
ORDER BY b.event_type;

-- Pivot rulings by id into one row per id with three UUID columns.
-- This version pre-aggregates duplicate (id, uuid) pairs to satisfy crosstab's uniqueness requirement.
SELECT
  ct.id,
  ct.card1_ruling,
  ct.card2_ruling,
  ct.card3_ruling
FROM crosstab(
  $$
    SELECT
      r.id::int AS id,
      r.uuid::text AS uuid,
      STRING_AGG(r."text", E'\n' ORDER BY r."text") AS ruling_text
    FROM ruling r
    WHERE r.uuid IN (
      '5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c',  -- card1
      '56f4935b-f6c5-59b9-88bf-9bcce20247ce',  -- card2
      '6d268c95-c176-5766-9a46-c14f739aba1c'   -- card3
    )
    GROUP BY r.id, r.uuid
    ORDER BY r.id, r.uuid
  $$,
  $$
    VALUES
      ('5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c'::text), -- card1 (1st column)
      ('56f4935b-f6c5-59b9-88bf-9bcce20247ce'::text), -- card2 (2nd column)
      ('6d268c95-c176-5766-9a46-c14f739aba1c'::text)  -- card3 (3rd column)
  $$
) AS ct(
  id int,
  card1_ruling text,
  card2_ruling text,
  card3_ruling text
)
ORDER BY ct.id;

WITH valid_promos AS (
  SELECT product, discount_start, discount_end, promotion_id
  FROM promotions
  WHERE discount_start IS NOT NULL
    AND discount_end IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT DISTINCT p1.product, 'Periods Overlap!' AS remark
FROM valid_promos p1
JOIN valid_promos p2
  ON p1.product = p2.product
 AND p1.promotion_id <> p2.promotion_id
 AND p1.discount_start <= p2.discount_end
 AND p2.discount_start <= p1.discount_end

WITH bond_fallback AS (
  -- One representative bond_type per molecule to use when an atom's element is missing
  SELECT molecule_id, MIN(bond_type) AS bond_type
  FROM bond
  GROUP BY molecule_id
)
SELECT id, molecule_id, val
FROM (
  -- Atoms (prefer element; if missing, fall back to a representative bond_type for the molecule)
  SELECT
    a.atom_id AS id,
    a.molecule_id,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(a.element, ''), bf.bond_type) AS val
  FROM atom a
  LEFT JOIN bond_fallback bf
    ON bf.molecule_id = a.molecule_id

  UNION ALL

  -- Bonds (use bond_type)
  SELECT
    b.bond_id AS id,
    b.molecule_id,
    b.bond_type AS val
  FROM bond b
) x
ORDER BY molecule_id, id

SELECT
  m.molecule_id,
  m.label,
  a1.atom_id AS atom_id,
  a1.element AS element,
  a2.atom_id AS atom_id2,
  a2.element AS element2,
  c.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM molecule AS m
JOIN bond AS b
  ON b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
JOIN connected AS c
  ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
JOIN atom AS a1
  ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
 AND a1.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
JOIN atom AS a2
  ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
 AND a2.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
WHERE (m.label IS NULL OR m.label IN ('+', '-'))
  AND c.atom_id < c.atom_id2  -- keep each undirected connection once
ORDER BY m.molecule_id, a1.atom_id, c.bond_id;

tiers(start_qty, end_qty, rate) AS (

    (10, 12, 100)

  UNION ALL SELECT '3333', 'DUMMY'
),
"MAP_RECRF_ASSET_TYPE" AS (

  UNION ALL SELECT 'SW', 'NOTIONALDB', 1
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SW', 'FWDNOTLCR', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SW', 'FWDNOTLDR', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SW', 'SWOFFBALCR', 2
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SW', 'SWOFFBALDR', 2

WITH src(id, geom) AS (
  VALUES
    (1, 'LINESTRING(0 0, 0 10)'::geometry),
    (2, 'LINESTRING(0 10, 0 11)'::geometry),
    (3, 'LINESTRING(1 1, 0 5)'::geometry),
    (4, 'LINESTRING(1 1, 2 2)'::geometry)
)
SELECT
  a.id AS endpoint_line_id,      -- line that ends at the junction
  b.id AS through_line_id,       -- line that continues through the junction (not broken)
  ST_AsText(p.pt) AS junction_pt
FROM src a
JOIN src b
  ON a.id <> b.id
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT ST_StartPoint(a.geom) AS pt
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ST_EndPoint(a.geom)
) AS p
WHERE ST_Intersects(b.geom, p.pt)                    -- a's endpoint lies on b
  AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_StartPoint(b.geom), p.pt)     -- not at b's start (so interior of b)
  AND NOT ST_Equals(ST_EndPoint(b.geom), p.pt);      -- not at b's end (so interior of b)

-- Total time spent within working hours (09:00–18:00, Mon–Fri), excluding non-working time
-- Adjust the date window below as needed (range_end is exclusive)
WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    TIMESTAMPTZ '2024-10-01 00:00:00' AS range_start,
    TIMESTAMPTZ '2024-10-10 00:00:00' AS range_end
),
per_day AS (
  SELECT
    e.learner_code,
    e.resource_key,
    day,
    GREATEST(e.start_timestamp, p.range_start, day + INTERVAL '9 hours') AS seg_start,
    LEAST(e.end_timestamp, p.range_end, day + INTERVAL '18 hours') AS seg_end
  FROM event_usage e
  CROSS JOIN params p
  -- Only consider events that overlap the reporting window at all
  WHERE e.end_timestamp > p.range_start
    AND e.start_timestamp < p.range_end
  -- Expand each event into the set of days it could contribute to (clamped to the window)
  JOIN LATERAL generate_series(
    date_trunc('day', GREATEST(e.start_timestamp, p.range_start)),
    date_trunc('day', LEAST(e.end_timestamp - INTERVAL '1 microsecond',
                            p.range_end   - INTERVAL '1 microsecond')),
    INTERVAL '1 day'
  ) AS day ON TRUE
  -- Weekdays only (Mon=1 ... Sun=7)
  WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM day) BETWEEN 1 AND 5
)
SELECT
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  SUM(GREATEST(0, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (seg_end - seg_start))))::bigint AS total_time_spent
FROM per_day
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key;

  COALESCE(SUM(t.amount) FILTER (WHERE t.productid = 5), 0) AS amount_prod_5,
  COALESCE(SUM(t.amount) FILTER (WHERE t.productid = 9), 0) AS amount_prod_9
FROM "transactions_1k" AS t
GROUP BY t.customerid, t."date"

WITH RECURSIVE molecule_atom_counts AS (
  SELECT "molecule_id", COUNT(*) AS atom_count
  FROM "atom"
  GROUP BY "molecule_id"
), molecule_atoms_recursive AS (
  SELECT "molecule_id", 1 AS atom_number, atom_count
  FROM molecule_atom_counts
  UNION ALL
  SELECT mar."molecule_id", mar.atom_number + 1, mar.atom_count
  FROM molecule_atoms_recursive mar
  WHERE mar.atom_number + 1 <= mar.atom_count
)
SELECT "molecule_id", atom_number
FROM molecule_atoms_recursive
ORDER BY "molecule_id", atom_number;

WITH overall_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(buildupplayspeed) AS avg_speed
  FROM "team_attributes"
  WHERE buildupplayspeed IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  m.id,
  m.date,
  m.home_team_api_id,
  m.away_team_api_id,
  m.home_team_goal,
  m.away_team_goal,
  t_home.team_long_name AS home_team_name,
  t_away.team_long_name AS away_team_name,
  ta_home.buildupplayspeed AS home_speed,
  oa.avg_speed AS overall_avg_speed
FROM "match" m
JOIN "team" t_home ON t_home.team_api_id = m.home_team_api_id
JOIN "team" t_away ON t_away.team_api_id = m.away_team_api_id
JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT ta.buildupplayspeed
  FROM "team_attributes" ta
  WHERE ta.team_api_id = m.home_team_api_id
    AND ta.date::timestamp <= m.date::timestamp
  ORDER BY ta.date::timestamp DESC
  LIMIT 1
) ta_home ON true
CROSS JOIN overall_avg oa
WHERE m.date::timestamp >= TIMESTAMP '2015-01-01'
  AND m.date::timestamp < TIMESTAMP '2016-01-01'
  AND ta_home.buildupplayspeed > oa.avg_speed
ORDER BY m.home_team_goal DESC, m.away_team_goal ASC;

FROM card AS c
WHERE c.issued IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY c.issued, c.card_id;

-- Extract each full timestamp from the space-separated string into its own row
-- (robust to the internal space between date and time)
SELECT t.id,
       m[1]::timestamp AS date_part
FROM team_attributes AS t
CROSS JOIN LATERAL regexp_matches(
  t.date,
  '(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})',
  'g'
) AS m;

WITH m AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    ("date")::timestamp AS start_ts,
    ("date")::timestamp + interval '2 hours' AS end_ts
  FROM "match"
),
b AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    start_ts,
    end_ts,
    date_trunc('day', start_ts) + interval '7 hours 30 minutes' AS business_start,
    date_trunc('day', start_ts) + interval '17 hours' AS business_end
  FROM m
)
SELECT
  b.id,
  b.start_ts AS match_start_time,
  b.end_ts AS match_end_time,
  GREATEST(
    ROUND(
      EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (LEAST(b.end_ts, b.business_end) - GREATEST(b.start_ts, b.business_start))) / 60.0
    ),
    0
  )::int AS match_duration_minutes
FROM b
WHERE EXTRACT(DOW FROM b.start_ts) BETWEEN 1 AND 5;

WITH params AS (
  SELECT
    TIMESTAMP '2024-09-04 00:00:00' AS start_ts,
    TIMESTAMP '2024-09-04 00:05:00' AS end_ts
),
school_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.cdscode AS "pk",
    CASE
      WHEN s.charter = 1 THEN 'Charter'
      WHEN s.magnet = 1 THEN 'Magnet'
      ELSE 'Regular'
    END AS "col2",
    -- Safer than POSITION/SUBSTRING when there's no space
    split_part(s.county, ' ', 1) AS "col3",
    to_timestamp(
      to_char(s.lastupdate, 'YYYY-MM-DD') || ' ' ||
      lpad(cast(mod(row_number() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode), 24) AS text), 2, '0') || ':' ||
      lpad(cast(mod(row_number() OVER (ORDER BY s.cdscode) * 7, 60) AS text), 2, '0'),
      'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI'
    )::timestamp AS "col4"
  FROM schools s
  WHERE s.statustype = 'Active'
    AND s.lastupdate IS NOT NULL
),
filtered_data AS (
  SELECT
    sd."pk",
    sd."col2",
    sd."col3",
    sd."col4"
  FROM school_data sd
  CROSS JOIN params p
  WHERE sd."col4" >= p.start_ts
    AND sd."col4" <  p.end_ts  -- half-open window [start, end)
)
SELECT
  fd."col2",
  -- Unique, ordered list of descriptive strings for the filtered window
  STRING_AGG(DISTINCT fd."col3", ', ' ORDER BY fd."col3") AS "col3",
  COUNT(fd."pk") AS "record_count"
FROM filtered_data fd
GROUP BY fd."col2"
ORDER BY "record_count" DESC;

WITH latest_attr AS (

  WHERE pa.player_api_id IS NOT NULL
  ORDER BY pa.player_api_id, pa.date::timestamp DESC, pa.id DESC
)
SELECT
  p.player_api_id AS player_id,
  p.player_name AS name,
  la.rating,
  la.preferred_foot
FROM player p
JOIN latest_attr la
  ON la.player_api_id = p.player_api_id

  WHERE 'paper' = ANY (string_to_array(COALESCE(c.availability, ''), ','))

WITH bucketed AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN t.count < 21 THEN '0-20'
      WHEN t.count < 34 THEN '21-33'
      WHEN t.count < 64 THEN '34-63'
      ELSE '64+'
    END AS count_range,
    CASE
      WHEN t.count < 21 THEN 1
      WHEN t.count < 34 THEN 2
      WHEN t.count < 64 THEN 3
      ELSE 4
    END AS sort_order,
    t.count::numeric AS cnt
  FROM tags AS t
  WHERE t.count IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT count_range, AVG(cnt) AS avg_count
FROM bucketed
GROUP BY count_range, sort_order
ORDER BY sort_order;

WITH bounds AS (
  SELECT DATE '2000-01-01' AS start_date, DATE '2025-12-31' AS end_date
),
first_last AS (
  SELECT
    player_fifa_api_id AS id,
    MIN(date::date) AS first_date,
    MAX(date::date) AS last_date
  FROM player_attributes
  WHERE player_fifa_api_id IS NOT NULL
    AND date IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY player_fifa_api_id
)
-- Gap from analysis start to first recorded date (only if first_date > start_date)
SELECT
  fl.id,
  'Before First Date' AS gap_type,
  fl.first_date AS reference_date,
  (fl.first_date - b.start_date) AS gap_days
FROM first_last fl
CROSS JOIN bounds b
WHERE fl.first_date > b.start_date

UNION ALL

-- Gap from last recorded date to analysis end (only if last_date < end_date)
SELECT
  fl.id,
  'After Last Date' AS gap_type,
  fl.last_date AS reference_date,
  (b.end_date - fl.last_date) AS gap_days
FROM first_last fl
CROSS JOIN bounds b
WHERE fl.last_date < b.end_date

ORDER BY id, gap_type;

SELECT

salespeople AS (

WITH m AS (
  SELECT
    CAST("date" AS timestamp) AS ts,
    date_trunc('day', CAST("date" AS timestamp))::date AS match_day,
    date_trunc('day', CAST("date" AS timestamp)) AS day_start,
    "match_api_id"
  FROM "match"
),
targets AS (
  SELECT 8 AS target_hour, 'AM' AS period
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 20 AS target_hour, 'PM' AS period
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    m.match_day,
    t.period,
    m.match_api_id,
    ABS(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (m.ts - (m.day_start + (interval '1 hour' * t.target_hour))))) AS diff_seconds,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY m.match_day, t.period
      ORDER BY ABS(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (m.ts - (m.day_start + (interval '1 hour' * t.target_hour))))) ASC,
               m.match_api_id ASC
    ) AS rn
  FROM m
  CROSS JOIN targets t
)
SELECT
  match_day AS match_date,
  MAX(match_api_id) FILTER (WHERE period = 'AM' AND rn = 1) AS closest_to_08_match_api_id,
  MAX(match_api_id) FILTER (WHERE period = 'PM' AND rn = 1) AS closest_to_20_match_api_id
FROM ranked
GROUP BY match_day
ORDER BY match_day;

WITH team_matches AS (
  -- One row per team per match with that team's per-match goal difference
  SELECT
    m.id AS match_id,
    m.date::timestamp AS match_ts,
    m.season,
    m.home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    m.home_team_api_id AS home_team_api_id,
    m.away_team_api_id AS away_team_api_id,
    m.home_team_goal AS goals_for,
    m.away_team_goal AS goals_against,
    (m.home_team_goal - m.away_team_goal) AS match_goal_diff
  FROM "match" AS m
  WHERE m.home_team_goal IS NOT NULL AND m.away_team_goal IS NOT NULL

  UNION ALL

  SELECT
    m.id AS match_id,
    m.date::timestamp AS match_ts,
    m.season,
    m.away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    m.home_team_api_id AS home_team_api_id,
    m.away_team_api_id AS away_team_api_id,
    m.away_team_goal AS goals_for,
    m.home_team_goal AS goals_against,
    (m.away_team_goal - m.home_team_goal) AS match_goal_diff
  FROM "match" AS m
  WHERE m.home_team_goal IS NOT NULL AND m.away_team_goal IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  tm.match_id AS id,
  tm.match_ts AS match_date,
  tm.team_api_id,
  t.team_long_name AS team_name,
  tm.home_team_api_id,
  ht.team_long_name AS home_team_name,
  tm.away_team_api_id,
  at.team_long_name AS away_team_name,
  tm.goals_for,
  tm.goals_against,
  tm.match_goal_diff,
  SUM(tm.match_goal_diff) OVER (
    PARTITION BY tm.team_api_id
    ORDER BY tm.match_ts, tm.match_id
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
  ) AS cumulative_goal_difference
FROM team_matches tm
LEFT JOIN "team" t  ON t.team_api_id  = tm.team_api_id
LEFT JOIN "team" ht ON ht.team_api_id = tm.home_team_api_id
LEFT JOIN "team" at ON at.team_api_id = tm.away_team_api_id
ORDER BY tm.team_api_id, tm.match_ts, tm.match_id;

WITH RECURSIVE atom_hierarchy AS (
  -- seed with the starting atom
  SELECT
    'TR007_8'::text AS atom_id,         -- previous (from) atom
    'TR007_8'::text AS atom_id2,        -- current (to) atom
    NULL::text AS bond_id,
    0 AS level,
    ARRAY['TR007_8']::text[] AS visited
  UNION ALL
  -- walk to the next connected atom, avoiding cycles by tracking visited nodes
  SELECT
    c.atom_id AS atom_id,               -- previous (from) atom
    c.atom_id2 AS atom_id2,             -- current (to) atom
    c.bond_id,
    ah.level + 1 AS level,
    ah.visited || c.atom_id2
  FROM connected c
  JOIN atom_hierarchy ah
    ON c.atom_id = ah.atom_id2
  WHERE NOT (c.atom_id2 = ANY(ah.visited))
)
-- keep only the first (shortest) discovery for each reachable atom
SELECT DISTINCT ON (ah.atom_id2)
  ah.atom_id,
  ah.atom_id2,
  ah.bond_id,
  ah.level
FROM atom_hierarchy ah
WHERE ah.level > 0
ORDER BY ah.atom_id2, ah.level;

SELECT
  a1.molecule_id,
  a1.atom_id AS atom1_id,
  a1.element AS atom1_element,
  a2.atom_id AS atom2_id,
  a2.element AS atom2_element,
  c.bond_id,
  b.bond_type
FROM connected AS c
JOIN atom AS a1 ON a1.atom_id = c.atom_id
JOIN atom AS a2 ON a2.atom_id = c.atom_id2
LEFT JOIN bond AS b
  ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
 AND b.molecule_id = a1.molecule_id
WHERE a1.molecule_id = a2.molecule_id
  AND c.atom_id < c.atom_id2
ORDER BY a1.molecule_id, a1.atom_id, a2.atom_id;

WITH daily_revenue AS (
  SELECT
    date::date AS dt,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE date IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1
),
w AS (
  SELECT
    dt,
    total_amount,
    LAG(dt, 1) OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS prev1,
    LAG(dt, 2) OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS prev2,
    SUM(total_amount) OVER (ORDER BY dt ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS sum3,
    AVG(total_amount) OVER (ORDER BY dt ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS avg3
  FROM daily_revenue
)
SELECT
  dt AS date,
  total_amount,
  CASE
    WHEN prev1 = dt - 1 AND prev2 = dt - 2 THEN sum3
    ELSE NULL
  END AS moving_sum_3d,
  CASE
    WHEN prev1 = dt - 1 AND prev2 = dt - 2 THEN ROUND(avg3::numeric, 2)
    ELSE NULL
  END AS moving_avg_3d
FROM w
ORDER BY dt;

WITH RECURSIVE colored AS (
  -- Anchor: start from leaves (nodes without children). Leaves already have a color.
  SELECT s.id, s.parent_id, s.color
  FROM superhero2 s
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM superhero2 c WHERE c.parent_id = s.id)
    AND s.color IS NOT NULL

  UNION ALL

  -- Recursive step: color parents only when all their children are already colored.
  SELECT p.id, p.parent_id,
         CASE
           WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MIN(c.color)  -- one child or two children with same color
           ELSE 'gray'                                        -- two children with different colors
         END AS color
  FROM superhero2 p
  JOIN superhero2 ch     ON ch.parent_id = p.id   -- children of p
  JOIN colored c         ON c.id = ch.id          -- only colored children
  LEFT JOIN colored done ON done.id = p.id        -- avoid recoloring the same parent
  WHERE done.id IS NULL
    AND NOT EXISTS (                               -- wait until all children are colored
      SELECT 1
      FROM superhero2 ch2
      WHERE ch2.parent_id = p.id
        AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM colored c2 WHERE c2.id = ch2.id)
    )
  GROUP BY p.id, p.parent_id
)
UPDATE superhero2 s
SET color = col.color
FROM colored col
WHERE s.id = col.id;

create or replace function setup_vector_store(p_table_name text, p_dimension int default 1536)
returns void
language plpgsql
as $$
declare
  v_match_fn_name text := format('%I_match_strategies', p_table_name);
begin
  -- Ensure pgvector is available
  execute 'create extension if not exists vector';

  -- Create the table (if it doesn't already exist)
  execute format(
    'create table if not exists %I (
       id bigserial primary key,
       embedding vector(%s) not null,
       description text,
       metadata jsonb not null default ''{}''::jsonb
     )',
     p_table_name, p_dimension
  );

  -- Create an IVFFlat index for efficient vector search (cosine distance)
  execute format(
    'create index if not exists %I_embedding_ivfflat
       on %I using ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)',
    p_table_name, p_table_name
  );

  -- Drop an existing search function for this table (if any)
  execute format('drop function if exists %I(vector, int, jsonb)', v_match_fn_name);

  -- Create a table-scoped search function that does vector similarity search
  execute format(
    'create function %I (
       query_embedding vector,
       match_count int default 10,
       filter jsonb default ''{}''::jsonb
     )
     returns table (
       id bigint,
       description text,
       metadata jsonb,
       similarity double precision
     )
     language plpgsql
     as $func$
     begin
       return query
       select
         id,
         description,
         metadata,
         1 - (embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity
       from %I
       where metadata @> coalesce(filter, ''{}''::jsonb)
       order by embedding <=> query_embedding
       limit coalesce(match_count, 10);
     end
     $func$;',
     v_match_fn_name, p_table_name
  );
end;
$$;

WITH pairs AS (

    a2.link_to_member AS member2_id,

      PARTITION BY a1.link_to_member, a2.link_to_member
      ORDER BY e.event_date::timestamp DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM attendance AS a1
  JOIN attendance AS a2
    ON a1.link_to_event = a2.link_to_event
   AND a1.link_to_member < a2.link_to_member
  JOIN event AS e
    ON a1.link_to_event = e.event_id
  WHERE e.event_date IS NOT NULL
),
last_per_pair AS (
  SELECT member1_id, member2_id, event_ts AS last_shared_event
  FROM pairs
  WHERE rn = 1
)
SELECT

  m1.first_name AS member1_first_name,

  l.member2_id,
  m2.first_name AS member2_first_name,

  l.last_shared_event
FROM last_per_pair l
JOIN member m1 ON m1.member_id = l.member1_id
JOIN member m2 ON m2.member_id = l.member2_id
ORDER BY l.last_shared_event ASC, l.member1_id, l.member2_id

  l.rnp, l.sm, l.sc170, l.ssa, l.ssb, l.centromea, l.dna, l."DNA-II"

-- Propagate each node's own emission up to all of its ancestors,
-- multiplying by the child->parent percentage on each step.
WITH RECURSIVE contrib AS (
  -- Base: each node contributes its own emission (weight = 1) to itself
  SELECT
    entityid AS src,
    entityid AS anc,
    COALESCE(emission, 0)::numeric AS emission,
    1.0::numeric AS weight
  FROM tree_table

  UNION ALL

  -- Step: move one level up (anc -> parent), applying the child's pctcontribution
  SELECT
    c.src,
    t.parentid AS anc,
    c.emission,
    c.weight * (COALESCE(t.pctcontribution, 0)::numeric / 100.0)
  FROM contrib c
  JOIN tree_table t
    ON t.entityid = c.anc
  WHERE t.parentid IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  a.entityid,
  a.parentid,
  SUM(c.emission * c.weight) AS total_emission
FROM contrib c
JOIN tree_table a
  ON a.entityid = c.anc
GROUP BY a.entityid, a.parentid
ORDER BY a.entityid;

    split_part("element", '_', 1) as student_id,
    lower(split_part("element", '_', 2)) as grade_type,

),
rates as (
  select
    student_id,
    max("record") filter (where grade_type = 'lowgrade')  as lowgrade,

  from parts
  group by student_id
)

from "frpm_record"
where lower(split_part("element", '_', 2)) in ('lowgrade', 'highgrade')
union all
select
  student_id || '_rate' as "element",
  lowgrade / nullif(highgrade, 0) as "record"
from rates
where lowgrade is not null
  and highgrade is not null
  and nullif(highgrade, 0) is not null;

WITH a(test) AS (
  VALUES
    ('Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16|DY7009|Address at some where|details'),
    ('Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16|DY7009|'),
    ('Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16')
)
SELECT
  test,
  array_to_string(string_to_array(test, '|')[1:5], '|') AS res
FROM a;

WITH ordered AS (
  SELECT
    l.id,
    l.date,
    l.got,
    COUNT(l.got) OVER (
      PARTITION BY l.id
      ORDER BY l.date
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS grp
  FROM laboratory l
)
SELECT
  id,
  date,
  got,
  MAX(got) OVER (PARTITION BY id, grp) AS new_got
FROM ordered
ORDER BY id, date;

WITH "credits" AS (
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        12 AS "ARTCRED",
        7.5 AS "BIZCRED",
        12 AS "HUMCRED",
        14 AS "NATCRED",
        11 AS "TEKCRED",
        9 AS "GENCRED",
        TO_DATE('01.01.2010', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "EFFECTIVE_DATE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        15 AS "ARTCRED",
        9 AS "BIZCRED",
        14 AS "HUMCRED",
        16 AS "NATCRED",
        13 AS "TEKCRED",
        11 AS "GENCRED",
        TO_DATE('01.01.2020', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "EFFECTIVE_DATE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5002 AS "ID",
        10.5 AS "ARTCRED",
        6 AS "BIZCRED",
        5 AS "HUMCRED",
        4 AS "NATCRED",
        6 AS "TEKCRED",
        3 AS "GENCRED",
        TO_DATE('01.01.2015', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "EFFECTIVE_DATE"
),
"grp_choices" AS (
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('03.03.2010', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'BIZ' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('05.05.2015', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5001 AS "ID",
        'NAT' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('23.06.2022', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        5002 AS "ID",
        'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE",
        TO_DATE('23.06.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS "DATECHOICE"
),
"credits_long" AS (
    SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'ART' AS "GROUPCHOICE", C."ARTCRED" AS "CREDIT_VALUE" FROM "credits" C
    UNION ALL SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'BIZ', C."BIZCRED" FROM "credits" C
    UNION ALL SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'HUM', C."HUMCRED" FROM "credits" C
    UNION ALL SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'NAT', C."NATCRED" FROM "credits" C
    UNION ALL SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'TEK', C."TEKCRED" FROM "credits" C
    UNION ALL SELECT C."ID", C."EFFECTIVE_DATE", 'GEN', C."GENCRED" FROM "credits" C
)
SELECT
    G."ID",
    G."GROUPCHOICE",
    G."DATECHOICE",
    X."CREDIT_VALUE"
FROM "grp_choices" G
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT CL."CREDIT_VALUE"
    FROM "credits_long" CL
    WHERE CL."ID" = G."ID"
      AND CL."GROUPCHOICE" = G."GROUPCHOICE"
      AND CL."EFFECTIVE_DATE" <= G."DATECHOICE"
    ORDER BY CL."EFFECTIVE_DATE" DESC
    LIMIT 1
) X ON true
ORDER BY G."ID", G."DATECHOICE", G."GROUPCHOICE";

WITH tbl AS (
  SELECT TO_DATE('12.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy') AS act_date
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('12.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('31.01.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('13.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('17.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('17.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('19.02.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('10.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('12.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('27.03.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
  UNION ALL SELECT TO_DATE('10.04.2023', 'dd.mm.yyyy')
),
-- Bucket every activation into its month
month_events AS (
  SELECT date_trunc('month', act_date)::date AS month_start
  FROM tbl
),
-- Distinct months present in the data
months AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT month_start FROM month_events
),
-- Count of items per month (each row in tbl represents one item)
monthly_counts AS (
  SELECT month_start, COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM month_events
  GROUP BY month_start
),
-- Grid of (activation_month, actual_month) where actual_month >= activation_month
grid AS (
  SELECT m1.month_start AS activation_month,
         m2.month_start AS actual_month
  FROM months m1
  JOIN months m2
    ON m2.month_start >= m1.month_start
)
-- Sum counts across the range; this also correctly handles the single-month case
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(g.activation_month, 'MON-YY') AS activation_month,
  TO_CHAR(g.actual_month, 'MON-YY')     AS actual_month,
  SUM(mc.cnt)                           AS active_items
FROM grid g
JOIN monthly_counts mc
  ON mc.month_start BETWEEN g.activation_month AND g.actual_month
GROUP BY g.activation_month, g.actual_month
ORDER BY g.activation_month, g.actual_month;
